question.english: What types of people influence the young in your country?
question.chinese: 在你國家，哪些類型的人會影響年輕人？

source_lines.text: Topic: What types of people influence the young in your country?
在你國家，哪些類型的人會影響年輕人？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I think the people who influence young people most are still parents and teachers, especially when it comes to values, study habits, and major life decisions.
components.chinese: 在香港，我認為對年輕人影響最大的，仍然是父母和老師，特別是在價值觀、學習習慣，以及重大人生決定方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I think the people who influence young people most are
still parents and teachers, especially when it comes to values, study habits, and
major life decisions.
[在香港，我認為對年輕人影響最大的，仍然是父母和老師，特別是在價值觀、學習習慣，以
及重大人生決定方面。]

components.english: The main reason is that parents and teachers are usually the adults who are closest to young people during their formative years. Parents shape the home environment, set expectations, and often have a strong say in matters like discipline, education, and future plans. Teachers, on the other hand, can leave a deep mark because they do not just teach subjects; they often shape confidence, attitude, and ambition as well. In a place like Hong Kong, where education carries a lot of weight, those voices can really set the tone.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，在年輕人成長的關鍵時期，父母和老師通常就是最接近他們的成年人。父母會塑造家庭環境、訂立期望，而且在紀律、教育，以及未來規劃這些事情上，往往有很大的話語權。至於老師，他們留下的影響也可以很深，因為他們教的從來不只是科目，很多時候也在塑造學生的自信、態度和志向。在像香港這樣很重視教育的地方，這些聲音很多時真的能替年輕人的方向定下基調。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that parents and teachers are usually the
adults who are closest to young people during their formative years. Parents
shape the home environment, set expectations, and often have a strong say in
matters like discipline, education, and future plans. Teachers, on the other hand,
can leave a deep mark because they do not just teach subjects; they often shape
confidence, attitude, and ambition as well. In a place like Hong Kong, where
education carries a lot of weight, those voices can really set the tone.
[主要原因是，在年輕人成長的關鍵時期，父母和老師通常就是最接近他們的成年人。父母
會塑造家庭環境、訂立期望，而且在紀律、教育，以及未來規劃這些事情上，往往有很大的
話語權。至於老師，他們留下的影響也可以很深，因為他們教的從來不只是科目，很多時候
也在塑造學生的自信、態度和志向。在像香港這樣很重視教育的地方，這些聲音很多時真的
能替年輕人的方向定下基調。]

components.english: For example, if a student grows up in a family that values academic success and has a teacher who constantly encourages them, that young person is quite likely to take school seriously and aim high. In that case, the influence is not loud, but it runs deep.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生成長於一個很重視學業成就的家庭，同時又遇上一位不斷鼓勵自己的老師，那麼這個年輕人很可能就會認真看待讀書，也更願意把目標訂得高一些。在這種情況下，那種影響未必很張揚，但卻非常深。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a student grows up in a family that values academic
success and has a teacher who constantly encourages them, that young person is
quite likely to take school seriously and aim high. In that case, the influence is
not loud, but it runs deep.
[例如，如果一個學生成長於一個很重視學業成就的家庭，同時又遇上一位不斷鼓勵自己的
老師，那麼這個年輕人很可能就會認真看待讀書，也更願意把目標訂得高一些。在這種情況
下，那種影響未必很張揚，但卻非常深。]

components.english: So, one major source of influence on the young in Hong Kong is still the traditional one: the adults who guide them at home and at school.
components.chinese: 所以，香港年輕人其中一個最主要的影響來源，仍然是比較傳統的那一類，也就是在家裡和學校裡引導他們的成年人。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major source of influence on the young in Hong Kong is
still the traditional one: the adults who guide them at home and at school.
[所以，香港年輕人其中一個最主要的影響來源，仍然是比較傳統的那一類，也就是在家裡
和學校裡引導他們的成年人。]

components.english: That said, peers, celebrities, and especially online influencers now play a huge role too, particularly in shaping taste, lifestyle, and self-image.
components.chinese: 不過，朋輩、名人，尤其是網上的意見領袖，如今也扮演著很大的角色，特別是在審美、生活方式，以及自我形象方面。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, peers, celebrities, and especially online influencers now play
a huge role too, particularly in shaping taste, lifestyle, and self-image.
[不過，朋輩、名人，尤其是網上的意見領袖，如今也扮演著很大的角色，特別是在審美、生
活方式，以及自我形象方面。]

components.english: To be honest, young people do not grow up only in the classroom or at the dinner table anymore; they also grow up on screens. Friends often influence the way they speak, dress, and think about what is “cool” or socially acceptable. At the same time, influencers, YouTubers, singers, and online personalities can shape trends at the speed of light. In other words, if parents and teachers plant the roots, the digital world often colours the leaves. It can broaden young people’s horizons, but it can also put pressure on them to compare, copy, and chase an image that may not be real.
components.chinese: 老實說，現在的年輕人已經不只是在人生的課室裡，或者飯桌旁長大了；他們同時也是在螢幕上長大的。朋友很多時會影響他們說話的方式、穿衣風格，以及他們對甚麼是「有型」或被社會接受的看法。與此同時，網紅、YouTuber、歌手，以及各種網上人物，也能以快得驚人的速度帶動潮流。換句話說，如果父母和老師是在種根，那麼數碼世界很多時候就是在替那些葉子上色。它固然可以幫助年輕人擴闊眼界，但同時也可能令他們陷入比較、模仿，以及追逐一些未必真實的形象之中。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, young people do not grow up only in the
classroom or at the dinner table anymore; they also grow up on screens. Friends
often influence the way they speak, dress, and think about what is “cool” or
socially acceptable. At the same time, influencers, YouTubers, singers, and online
personalities can shape trends at the speed of light. In other words, if parents
and teachers plant the roots, the digital world often colours the leaves. It can
broaden young people’s horizons, but it can also put pressure on them to
compare, copy, and chase an image that may not be real.
[老實說，現在的年輕人已經不只是在人生的課室裡，或者飯桌旁長大了；他們同時也是在
螢幕上長大的。朋友很多時會影響他們說話的方式、穿衣風格，以及他們對甚麼是「有型」或
被社會接受的看法。與此同時，網紅、YouTuber、歌手，以及各種網上人物，也能以快得驚
人的速度帶動潮流。換句話說，如果父母和老師是在種根，那麼數碼世界很多時候就是在替
那些葉子上色。它固然可以幫助年輕人擴闊眼界，但同時也可能令他們陷入比較、模仿，以
及追逐一些未必真實的形象之中。]

components.english: For instance, a teenager in Hong Kong may still listen to their parents about exam choices, but when it comes to fashion, slang, music, fitness, or beauty standards, they are often far more influenced by friends or social media personalities. That is where the pull of modern influence becomes impossible to ignore.
components.chinese: 例如，一個香港青少年在選科或考試安排上，也許仍然會聽父母的意見；但一談到穿衣風格、用語、音樂、健身，或者審美標準時，他往往就更容易受到朋友或社交媒體人物影響。這也正是現代影響力變得令人無法忽視的地方。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teenager in Hong Kong may still listen to their
parents about exam choices, but when it comes to fashion, slang, music, fitness,
or beauty standards, they are often far more influenced by friends or social
media personalities. That is where the pull of modern influence becomes
impossible to ignore.
[例如，一個香港青少年在選科或考試安排上，也許仍然會聽父母的意見；但一談到穿衣風
格、用語、音樂、健身，或者審美標準時，他往往就更容易受到朋友或社交媒體人物影響。這
也正是現代影響力變得令人無法忽視的地方。]

components.english: Overall, I would say the young in Hong Kong are influenced by both traditional authority figures and modern social figures. Parents and teachers still shape the backbone, but peers and the online world often shape the surface, the mood, and sometimes even the mirror through which young people see themselves.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港的年輕人同時受到傳統權威人物和現代社會人物的影響。父母和老師仍然在塑造骨架，但朋輩和網絡世界很多時則在塑造表面、情緒，甚至那面讓年輕人看待自己的鏡子。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say the young in Hong Kong are influenced by
both traditional authority figures and modern social figures. Parents and
teachers still shape the backbone, but peers and the online world often shape
the surface, the mood, and sometimes even the mirror through which young
people see themselves.
[總結來說，我會說香港的年輕人同時受到傳統權威人物和現代社會人物的影響。父母和老
師仍然在塑造骨架，但朋輩和網絡世界很多時則在塑造表面、情緒，甚至那面讓年輕人看待
自己的鏡子。]

question.english: What type of people, such as parents, teachers, or friends, are best to influence young people’s behaviour?
question.chinese: 像父母、老師或朋友這類人當中，哪一類最適合影響年輕人的行為？

source_lines.text: Topic: What type of people, such as parents, teachers, or friends, are best to
influence young people’s behaviour?
像父母、老師或朋友這類人當中，哪一類最適合影響年輕人的行為？

components.english: If I had to choose, I would say parents are usually the best people to influence young people’s behaviour, especially in the long run.
components.chinese: 如果一定要我選，我會說父母通常是最適合影響年輕人行為的人，尤其從長遠來看更是如此。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If I had to choose, I would say parents are usually the best people to
influence young people’s behaviour, especially in the long run.
[如果一定要我選，我會說父母通常是最適合影響年輕人行為的人，尤其從長遠來看更是如

components.english: The main reason is that parents are there from the very beginning. They do not just give advice now and then; they shape a child’s world day by day, year by year. Young people often learn behaviour not from what adults preach, but from what they practise. If parents are respectful, responsible, and emotionally steady, those habits tend to sink in quietly and take root. In that sense, parents do not merely guide behaviour; they help build the moral backbone behind it.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，父母從一開始就在孩子身邊。他們不只是偶爾給幾句建議，而是日復一日、年復一年地塑造孩子所處的世界。年輕人很多時候學到的，並不是成年人口頭上教了甚麼，而是他們實際做了甚麼。如果父母待人有禮、做事負責，而且情緒穩定，這些習慣往往就會安靜地滲進孩子心裡，慢慢扎根。從這個角度來看，父母不只是引導行為；他們其實是在替行為背後的品格打地基。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that parents are there from the very
beginning. They do not just give advice now and then; they shape a child’s
world day by day, year by year. Young people often learn behaviour not from
what adults preach, but from what they practise. If parents are respectful,
responsible, and emotionally steady, those habits tend to sink in quietly and
take root. In that sense, parents do not merely guide behaviour; they help build
the moral backbone behind it.
[主要原因是，父母從一開始就在孩子身邊。他們不只是偶爾給幾句建議，而是日復一日、
年復一年地塑造孩子所處的世界。年輕人很多時候學到的，並不是成年人口頭上教了甚麼，
而是他們實際做了甚麼。如果父母待人有禮、做事負責，而且情緒穩定，這些習慣往往就會
安靜地滲進孩子心裡，慢慢扎根。從這個角度來看，父母不只是引導行為；他們其實是在替
行為背後的品格打地基。]

components.english: For example, if a child grows up watching their parents keep promises, speak politely to others, and stay calm during conflict, that child is much more likely to absorb those behaviours naturally. The lesson lands not because it is loud, but because it is lived.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子在成長過程中，看著父母守承諾、待人有禮，以及在衝突中保持冷靜，那麼這個孩子就很可能會很自然地吸收這些行為模式。那份教導之所以有效，不是因為它說得大聲，而是因為它被活了出來。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child grows up watching their parents keep
promises, speak politely to others, and stay calm during conflict, that child is
much more likely to absorb those behaviours naturally. The lesson lands not
because it is loud, but because it is lived.
[例如，如果一個孩子在成長過程中，看著父母守承諾、待人有禮，以及在衝突中保持冷靜，
那麼這個孩子就很可能會很自然地吸收這些行為模式。那份教導之所以有效，不是因為它
說得大聲，而是因為它被活了出來。]

components.english: So, overall, I think parents are the strongest influence, because they shape both behaviour and the values underneath it.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我認為父母是最強的影響來源，因為他們塑造的不只是行為本身，還包括行為底下的價值觀。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I think parents are the strongest influence, because
they shape both behaviour and the values underneath it.
[所以，整體來說，我認為父母是最強的影響來源，因為他們塑造的不只是行為本身，還包
括行為底下的價值觀。]

components.english: That said, teachers can also be extremely important, and in some cases they may influence behaviour more effectively than parents in specific areas.
components.chinese: 不過，老師同樣也可以非常重要，而且在某些範疇上，他們甚至可能比父母更有效地影響行為。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea: That said, teachers can also be extremely important, and in some cases
they may influence behaviour more effectively than parents in specific areas.
[不過，老師同樣也可以非常重要，而且在某些範疇上，他們甚至可能比父母更有效地影響
行為。]

components.english: Teachers often have a different kind of authority. They are slightly more distant than parents, so young people may listen to them with fresher ears. A good teacher can shape discipline, attitude, confidence, and the way students treat other people. Friends, of course, also have huge influence, especially in teenage years, but that influence is a double-edged sword. It can encourage kindness and belonging, or it can pull people in the wrong direction through peer pressure. So, if we are talking about the best influence rather than simply the strongest, I would still put parents first, with teachers close behind.
components.chinese: 老師很多時候擁有另一種權威。他們和父母相比，距離稍微遠一點，所以年輕人有時反而會以更新鮮的耳朵去聽他們的話。一位好老師能夠塑造紀律、態度、自信，以及學生如何對待別人。至於朋友，當然也有很大影響力，特別是在青春期時更是如此，但那種影響其實是一把雙刃劍。它可以鼓勵善良和歸屬感，也可以透過朋輩壓力把人拉向錯的方向。所以，如果我們談的是「最好的影響」，而不只是「最強的影響」，我仍然會把父母放在第一位，而老師則緊隨其後。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Teachers often have a different kind of authority. They are
slightly more distant than parents, so young people may listen to them with
fresher ears. A good teacher can shape discipline, attitude, confidence, and the
way students treat other people. Friends, of course, also have huge influence,
especially in teenage years, but that influence is a double-edged sword. It can
encourage kindness and belonging, or it can pull people in the wrong direction
through peer pressure. So, if we are talking about the best influence rather than
simply the strongest, I would still put parents first, with teachers close behind.
[老師很多時候擁有另一種權威。他們和父母相比，距離稍微遠一點，所以年輕人有時反而
會以更新鮮的耳朵去聽他們的話。一位好老師能夠塑造紀律、態度、自信，以及學生如何對
待別人。至於朋友，當然也有很大影響力，特別是在青春期時更是如此，但那種影響其實是
一把雙刃劍。它可以鼓勵善良和歸屬感，也可以透過朋輩壓力把人拉向錯的方向。所以，如
果我們談的是「最好的影響」，而不只是「最強的影響」，我仍然會把父母放在第一位，而老師
則緊隨其後。]

components.english: For instance, a teenager may ignore their parents on a particular day, but a respected teacher’s words about honesty, effort, or responsibility may suddenly strike a chord. Even so, if the values at home are weak, that influence may not last. That is why teachers can refine behaviour, but parents usually set the deeper pattern.
components.chinese: 例如，一個青少年某一天也許會不聽父母的話，但一位受他尊敬的老師談到誠實、努力，或責任感時，卻可能一下子打動他。即便如此，如果家庭裡的價值基礎本身很薄弱，那種影響很多時也未必能長久。這就是為甚麼老師能夠修正和提升行為，但父母通常才是在設定更深層的模式。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teenager may ignore their parents on a particular
day, but a respected teacher’s words about honesty, effort, or responsibility may
suddenly strike a chord. Even so, if the values at home are weak, that influence
may not last. That is why teachers can refine behaviour, but parents usually set

更深層的模式。]

components.english: Overall, I would say parents are the best people to influence young people’s behaviour, while teachers are a very strong second. Friends shape behaviour too, but often in more changeable ways. In simple terms, parents lay the foundation, teachers strengthen the structure, and friends test it in the real world.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說父母是最適合影響年輕人行為的人，而老師則是非常重要的第二位。朋友當然也會影響行為，但那種影響很多時更容易變動。簡單來說，父母負責打地基，老師負責把結構加固，而朋友則是在真實世界裡不斷測試那個結構。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say parents are the best people to influence
young people’s behaviour, while teachers are a very strong second. Friends shape
behaviour too, but often in more changeable ways. In simple terms, parents lay
the foundation, teachers strengthen the structure, and friends test it in the real
world.
[總結來說，我會說父母是最適合影響年輕人行為的人，而老師則是非常重要的第二位。朋
友當然也會影響行為，但那種影響很多時更容易變動。簡單來說，父母負責打地基，老師負
責把結構加固，而朋友則是在真實世界裡不斷測試那個結構。]

question.english: Why is it important for young people to have role models?
question.chinese: 為甚麼年輕人有榜樣是重要的？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why is it important for young people to have role models?
為甚麼年輕人有榜樣是重要的？

components.english: I think it is very important, because role models can give young people direction and show them what is actually possible in life.
components.chinese: 我認為這是很重要的，因為榜樣能給年輕人方向感，也能讓他們看見人生裡甚麼是真正有可能做到的。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think it is very important, because role models can give young people
direction and show them what is actually possible in life.
[我認為這是很重要的，因為榜樣能給年輕人方向感，也能讓他們看見人生裡甚麼是真正有
可能做到的。]

components.english: When people are young, they are still figuring out who they are, what they value, and the kind of person they want to become. At that stage, a good role model can act like a compass. They do not simply tell young people what to do; they show them, through real behaviour, what qualities like discipline, courage, kindness, or perseverance actually look like. In that sense, a role model turns abstract advice into something living and visible.
components.chinese: 當一個人還年輕時，他通常仍然在摸索自己是怎樣的人、重視甚麼，以及想成為甚麼樣的人。在這個階段，一個好的榜樣就像指南針一樣。他們不只是告訴年輕人應該做甚麼，而是透過真實行為，讓年輕人看見自律、勇氣、善良，或者堅持這些特質到底是甚麼模樣。從這個角度來看，榜樣能把抽象的道理，變成有生命、看得見的東西。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people are young, they are still figuring out who they are,
what they value, and the kind of person they want to become. At that stage, a
good role model can act like a compass. They do not simply tell young people
what to do; they show them, through real behaviour, what qualities like
discipline, courage, kindness, or perseverance actually look like. In that sense, a
role model turns abstract advice into something living and visible.
[當一個人還年輕時，他通常仍然在摸索自己是怎樣的人、重視甚麼，以及想成為甚麼樣的
人。在這個階段，一個好的榜樣就像指南針一樣。他們不只是告訴年輕人應該做甚麼，而是
透過真實行為，讓年輕人看見自律、勇氣、善良，或者堅持這些特質到底是甚麼模樣。從這
個角度來看，榜樣能把抽象的道理，變成有生命、看得見的東西。]

components.english: For example, if a teenager admires a teacher who is patient, hardworking, and fair, that student may begin to copy those qualities without even realising it. In the same way, if a young athlete looks up to someone who trains seriously and stays humble, they are more likely to understand that success is built on effort rather than empty talent alone.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位青少年很欣賞一位有耐性、勤力，而且公平的老師，那位學生可能會在不知不覺間開始模仿那些特質。同樣地，如果一位年輕運動員很敬佩一位訓練認真而且保持謙遜的人，他就更有可能明白，成功並不是單靠天分，而是建立在努力之上的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a teenager admires a teacher who is patient,
hardworking, and fair, that student may begin to copy those qualities without
even realising it. In the same way, if a young athlete looks up to someone who
trains seriously and stays humble, they are more likely to understand that
success is built on effort rather than empty talent alone.
[例如，如果一位青少年很欣賞一位有耐性、勤力，而且公平的老師，那位學生可能會在不
知不覺間開始模仿那些特質。同樣地，如果一位年輕運動員很敬佩一位訓練認真而且保持
謙遜的人，他就更有可能明白，成功並不是單靠天分，而是建立在努力之上的。]

components.english: So, one major reason role models matter is that they give young people something solid to aim at, instead of leaving them to grope in the dark.
components.chinese: 所以，榜樣之所以重要，其中一個主要原因就是，他們能讓年輕人有一個實實在在可以努力的方向，而不是讓他們在黑暗中自己摸索。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major reason role models matter is that they give young
people something solid to aim at, instead of leaving them to grope in the dark.
[所以，榜樣之所以重要，其中一個主要原因就是，他們能讓年輕人有一個實實在在可以努
力的方向，而不是讓他們在黑暗中自己摸索。]

components.english: On top of that, role models can also give young people hope and motivation, especially when life feels difficult or uncertain.
components.chinese: 除此之外，榜樣也能帶給年輕人希望和動力，特別是在生活看起來困難或充滿不確定性的時候。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, role models can also give young people hope and
motivation, especially when life feels difficult or uncertain.
[除此之外，榜樣也能帶給年輕人希望和動力，特別是在生活看起來困難或充滿不確定性的
時候。]

components.english: Sometimes young people need more than advice; they need proof that setbacks can be overcome. A strong role model can show them that failure is not the end of the road, but often part of the road. That kind of example can be incredibly powerful. However, I do think there is one important condition: role models should inspire young people, not trap them in comparison. The point is not to copy someone else’s life line by line, but to learn from their character and apply it in your own way.
components.chinese: 有時候，年輕人需要的不只是忠告；他們更需要一種證明，證明挫折是可以跨過去的。一個有力量的榜樣能讓他們明白，失敗並不是路的盡頭，很多時反而就是路的一部分。這種示範其實非常有力量。不過，我也認為有一個重要前提：榜樣應該帶來啟發，而不是把年輕人困在比較之中。重點不是逐字逐句複製別人的人生，而是從對方的品格中學到東西，然後用自己的方式活出來。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Sometimes young people need more than advice; they need
proof that setbacks can be overcome. A strong role model can show them that
failure is not the end of the road, but often part of the road. That kind of
example can be incredibly powerful. However, I do think there is one important
condition: role models should inspire young people, not trap them in
comparison. The point is not to copy someone else’s life line by line, but to learn
from their character and apply it in your own way.
[有時候，年輕人需要的不只是忠告；他們更需要一種證明，證明挫折是可以跨過去的。一
個有力量的榜樣能讓他們明白，失敗並不是路的盡頭，很多時反而就是路的一部分。這種示
範其實非常有力量。不過，我也認為有一個重要前提：榜樣應該帶來啟發，而不是把年輕人
困在比較之中。重點不是逐字逐句複製別人的人生，而是從對方的品格中學到東西，然後用
自己的方式活出來。]

components.english: For instance, a young person from a difficult background may feel more determined if they hear about someone who faced similar obstacles and still moved forward. It could be a public figure, a coach, an older sibling, or even a parent. Sometimes the most powerful role model is not the one in the spotlight, but the one quietly showing up every day and doing the right thing.
components.chinese: 例如，一位來自困難背景的年輕人，如果聽到有人面對過相似障礙，最後仍然一步一步走下去，他通常就會更有決心。那個榜樣可以是公眾人物、教練、哥哥姐姐，甚至父母。有時最有力量的榜樣，未必是站在鎂光燈下的那一位，反而是那個每天安安靜靜出現、踏踏實實做對事情的人。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a young person from a difficult background may feel
more determined if they hear about someone who faced similar obstacles and
still moved forward. It could be a public figure, a coach, an older sibling, or even
a parent. Sometimes the most powerful role model is not the one in the
spotlight, but the one quietly showing up every day and doing the right thing.
[例如，一位來自困難背景的年輕人，如果聽到有人面對過相似障礙，最後仍然一步一步走
下去，他通常就會更有決心。那個榜樣可以是公眾人物、教練、哥哥姐姐，甚至父母。有時最
有力量的榜樣，未必是站在鎂光燈下的那一位，反而是那個每天安安靜靜出現、踏踏實實做
對事情的人。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say role models are very important for young people, because they can shape ambition, strengthen character, and make the future feel more reachable. A good role model does not live life for you; they simply light the path a little, so you can walk it with more courage of your own.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說榜樣對年輕人非常重要，因為他們能塑造志向、品格，並且讓未來看起來不會那麼遙遠。一個好的榜樣不會替你活你的人生；他們只是把前面的路稍微照亮一點，讓你能帶著更多屬於自己的勇氣走下去。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say role models are very important for young
people, because they can shape ambition, strengthen character, and make the
future feel more reachable. A good role model does not live life for you; they
simply light the path a little, so you can walk it with more courage of your own.
[總結來說，我會說榜樣對年輕人非常重要，因為他們能塑造志向、品格，並且讓未來看起
來不會那麼遙遠。一個好的榜樣不會替你活你的人生；他們只是把前面的路稍微照亮一點，
讓你能帶著更多屬於自己的勇氣走下去。]

question.english: What do you think young people will be influenced by the most in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來年輕人最受甚麼影響？

source_lines.text: Topic: What do you think young people will be influenced by the most in the future?
你認為未來年輕人最受甚麼影響？

components.english: Personally, I think young people will be influenced most by technology, especially digital media, social platforms, and increasingly AI-driven content.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為未來年輕人最受影響的，會是科技，尤其是數碼媒體、社交平台，以及愈來愈多由人工智能推動的內容。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think young people will be influenced most by technology,
especially digital media, social platforms, and increasingly AI-driven content.
[就我個人而言，我認為未來年輕人最受影響的，會是科技，尤其是數碼媒體、社交平台，以
及愈來愈多由人工智能推動的內容。]

components.english: The main reason is that technology is no longer just a tool sitting in the background; it is becoming the water young people swim in. It shapes what they watch, how they communicate, what they buy, what they admire, and even how they think about themselves. When someone spends hours a day online, the screen does not simply reflect their world; little by little, it helps build it. In that sense, digital influence can be quiet, constant, and incredibly powerful.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，科技已經不再只是靜靜放在背景裡的一件工具；它正逐漸變成年輕人成長其中的「環境本身」。它會影響他們看甚麼、如何溝通、買甚麼、欣賞甚麼，甚至如何看待自己。當一個人每天花很多時間在網上時，那塊螢幕不只是反映他的世界；它其實也在一點一滴地塑造那個世界。從這個角度來說，數碼影響力往往安靜、持續，而且非常強大。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that technology is no longer just a tool
sitting in the background; it is becoming the water young people swim in. It
shapes what they watch, how they communicate, what they buy, what they
admire, and even how they think about themselves. When someone spends
hours a day online, the screen does not simply reflect their world; little by little,
it helps build it. In that sense, digital influence can be quiet, constant, and
incredibly powerful.
[主要原因是，科技已經不再只是靜靜放在背景裡的一件工具；它正逐漸變成年輕人成長其
中的「環境本身」。它會影響他們看甚麼、如何溝通、買甚麼、欣賞甚麼，甚至如何看待自己。
當一個人每天花很多時間在網上時，那塊螢幕不只是反映他的世界；它其實也在一點一滴
地塑造那個世界。從這個角度來說，數碼影響力往往安靜、持續，而且非常強大。]

components.english: For example, a teenager may pick up fashion trends from short videos, develop opinions from online creators, learn study habits from educational content, and even shape career goals through what they see on social media. Sometimes they do not even realise how much of their taste and thinking is being nudged along.
components.chinese: 例如，一個青少年可能會從短影片裡吸收穿搭潮流，從網上創作者那裡形成某些看法，從教學內容裡學到讀書方法，甚至透過社交平台上的資訊去塑造自己的職業想像。有時候，他們甚至未必意識到，自己的品味和思考方式其實已經在不知不覺中被推動着。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a teenager may pick up fashion trends from short
videos, develop opinions from online creators, learn study habits from
educational content, and even shape career goals through what they see on social
media. Sometimes they do not even realise how much of their taste and thinking
is being nudged along.
[例如，一個青少年可能會從短影片裡吸收穿搭潮流，從網上創作者那裡形成某些看法，從
教學內容裡學到讀書方法，甚至透過社交平台上的資訊去塑造自己的職業想像。有時候，他
們甚至未必意識到，自己的品味和思考方式其實已經在不知不覺中被推動着。]

components.english: So, if I had to choose one main influence, I would say technology will probably have the strongest pull, because it is fast, immersive, and almost impossible to switch off completely.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要選一個最主要的影響來源，我會說科技大概會有最強的牽引力，因為它速度快、沉浸感強，而且幾乎不可能完全關掉。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to choose one main influence, I would say technology
will probably have the strongest pull, because it is fast, immersive, and almost
impossible to switch off completely.
[所以，如果一定要選一個最主要的影響來源，我會說科技大概會有最強的牽引力，因為它
速度快、沉浸感強，而且幾乎不可能完全關掉。]

components.english: That said, I do not think technology will be the whole story, because family, school, and real-life relationships will still matter enormously.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為科技會是全部，因為家庭、學校，以及真實生活中的人際關係，仍然會非常重要。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think technology will be the whole story, because
family, school, and real-life relationships will still matter enormously.
[不過，我並不認為科技會是全部，因為家庭、學校，以及真實生活中的人際關係，仍然會非
常重要。]

components.english: However strong online influence becomes, young people are still shaped by the people who know them personally and care about them deeply. A parent, a teacher, or a close friend can sometimes outweigh a hundred online voices, because real relationships carry trust, warmth, and accountability. In other words, the internet may set the mood, but family and community often set the moral compass. So the future influence on young people will probably be a tug-of-war between the digital world and the human one.
components.chinese: 無論網上的影響力變得多強，年輕人始終還是會被那些真正認識他們、也真心在乎他們的人所塑造。父母、老師，或者親密朋友，有時候的確能勝過網上上百種聲音，因為真實關係帶着信任、溫度，以及責任感。換句話說，互聯網也許能決定氣氛，但家庭和社區很多時候才是真正定下價值方向的指南針。所以，未來影響年輕人的力量，很可能會是一場數碼世界和人類世界之間的拉扯。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: However strong online influence becomes, young people are
still shaped by the people who know them personally and care about them
deeply. A parent, a teacher, or a close friend can sometimes outweigh a hundred
online voices, because real relationships carry trust, warmth, and accountability.
In other words, the internet may set the mood, but family and community often
set the moral compass. So the future influence on young people will probably be
a tug-of-war between the digital world and the human one.
[無論網上的影響力變得多強，年輕人始終還是會被那些真正認識他們、也真心在乎他們的
人所塑造。父母、老師，或者親密朋友，有時候的確能勝過網上上百種聲音，因為真實關係
帶着信任、溫度，以及責任感。換句話說，互聯網也許能決定氣氛，但家庭和社區很多時候
才是真正定下價值方向的指南針。所以，未來影響年輕人的力量，很可能會是一場數碼世界
和人類世界之間的拉扯。]

components.english: For instance, a young person may be heavily influenced by online trends, but if they grow up in a thoughtful family or attend a school that encourages critical thinking, they are much more likely to question what they see instead of swallowing it whole. That kind of grounding can make all the difference.
components.chinese: 例如，一個年輕人也許會很受網上潮流影響，但如果他成長於一個有思考的家庭，或者就讀一所鼓勵批判思考的學校，他就更有可能去質疑自己看到的東西，而不是照單全收。那種扎實的根基，很多時候真的能帶來很大分別。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a young person may be heavily influenced by online
trends, but if they grow up in a thoughtful family or attend a school that
encourages critical thinking, they are much more likely to question what they
see instead of swallowing it whole. That kind of grounding can make all the
difference.
[例如，一個年輕人也許會很受網上潮流影響，但如果他成長於一個有思考的家庭，或者就
讀一所鼓勵批判思考的學校，他就更有可能去質疑自己看到的東西，而不是照單全收。那種
扎實的根基，很多時候真的能帶來很大分別。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say young people in the future will be influenced most by technology, but whether that influence becomes helpful or harmful will depend a lot on the guidance they receive in real life. The loudest voice may come from the screen, but the wisest one should still come from people.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說未來年輕人最受影響的，仍然會是科技；不過，那種影響最後是變成助力還是阻力，很大程度上還是取決於他們在現實生活中得到怎樣的引導。最大的聲音也許來自螢幕，但最有智慧的聲音，始終應該來自人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say young people in the future will be influenced
most by technology, but whether that influence becomes helpful or harmful will
depend a lot on the guidance they receive in real life. The loudest voice may
come from the screen, but the wisest one should still come from people.
[總結來說，我會說未來年輕人最受影響的，仍然會是科技；不過，那種影響最後是變成助
力還是阻力，很大程度上還是取決於他們在現實生活中得到怎樣的引導。最大的聲音也許
來自螢幕，但最有智慧的聲音，始終應該來自人。]

question.english: Why is it important to have role models?
question.chinese: 為甚麼擁有榜樣是重要的？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why is it important to have role models?
為甚麼擁有榜樣是重要的？

components.english: Personally, I think it is very important to have role models, because they give people someone to look up to and show them what is possible.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為擁有榜樣是非常重要的，因為榜樣能讓人有一個可以仰望和學習的對象，也能讓人看見甚麼是有可能做到的。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think it is very important to have role models, because they
give people someone to look up to and show them what is possible.
[就我個人而言，我認為擁有榜樣是非常重要的，因為榜樣能讓人有一個可以仰望和學習的
對象，也能讓人看見甚麼是有可能做到的。]

components.english: When people hear general advice like “work hard” or “be kind,” it can sound a bit abstract. But when they see those qualities in a real person, the message suddenly lands. A role model makes good values feel more concrete and more reachable. In that sense, a good role model is a bit like a compass: they do not walk the road for you, but they help you keep your direction when life gets confusing.
components.chinese: 當人們只聽到一些很籠統的建議，例如「要努力」或者「要善良」時，那些話有時會顯得比較抽象。但當他們在一個真實的人身上看見這些特質時，那種訊息就會一下子變得更有力量。榜樣能令好的價值觀變得更具體，也更像是自己可以做到的事。從這個角度來看，一個好的榜樣有點像指南針：他不會替你走路，但當人生變得混亂時，他能幫你守住方向。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people hear general advice like “work hard” or “be kind,”
it can sound a bit abstract. But when they see those qualities in a real person,
the message suddenly lands. A role model makes good values feel more concrete
and more reachable. In that sense, a good role model is a bit like a compass: they
do not walk the road for you, but they help you keep your direction when life
gets confusing.
[當人們只聽到一些很籠統的建議，例如「要努力」或者「要善良」時，那些話有時會顯得比較
抽象。但當他們在一個真實的人身上看見這些特質時，那種訊息就會一下子變得更有力量。
榜樣能令好的價值觀變得更具體，也更像是自己可以做到的事。從這個角度來看，一個好的
榜樣有點像指南針：他不會替你走路，但當人生變得混亂時，他能幫你守住方向。]

components.english: For example, a student may admire a teacher who is patient, knowledgeable, and fair. Over time, that student may begin to copy not only the teacher’s study habits, but also the way the teacher treats other people. In that case, the role model is shaping both achievement and character.
components.chinese: 例如，一個學生可能會很欣賞一位有耐性、有知識，而且待人公平的老師。久而久之，那個學生開始模仿的，不只是老師的讀書習慣，也包括老師待人的方式。這種情況下，榜樣其實不只是影響一個人的成績，也在塑造一個人的品格。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a student may admire a teacher who is patient,
knowledgeable, and fair. Over time, that student may begin to copy not only the
teacher’s study habits, but also the way the teacher treats other people. In that
case, the role model is shaping both achievement and character.
[例如，一個學生可能會很欣賞一位有耐性、有知識，而且待人公平的老師。久而久之，那個
學生開始模仿的，不只是老師的讀書習慣，也包括老師待人的方式。這種情況下，榜樣其實
不只是影響一個人的成績，也在塑造一個人的品格。]

components.english: So, in my view, role models matter because they turn good ideas into something people can actually see, admire, and try to follow.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，榜樣之所以重要，是因為他們能把好的觀念變成一些人們真正看得見、會欣賞，也願意跟隨的東西。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in my view, role models matter because they turn good ideas
into something people can actually see, admire, and try to follow.
[所以，在我看來，榜樣之所以重要，是因為他們能把好的觀念變成一些人們真正看得見、
會欣賞，也願意跟隨的東西。]

components.english: That said, I do not think role models should be followed blindly, because no one is perfect.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為榜樣應該被盲目追隨，因為沒有人是完美的。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think role models should be followed blindly,
because no one is perfect.
[不過，我也不認為榜樣應該被盲目追隨，因為沒有人是完美的。]

components.english: A role model should inspire people, not replace their own judgment. If someone puts another person on too high a pedestal, they may feel disappointed the moment that person makes a mistake. The healthier approach, I think, is to learn from a role model’s strengths without copying every detail of their life. After all, the point is not to become someone else; it is to become a better version of yourself.
components.chinese: 榜樣應該帶來啟發，而不是取代一個人本身的判斷。如果一個人把另一個人放得太高、太神化，那麼對方一旦犯錯，他就很容易感到失望。我覺得更健康的做法，是向榜樣的優點學習，但不要把對方人生的每一個細節都照單全收。畢竟，重點不是把自己變成另一個人，而是讓自己成為一個更好的自己。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: A role model should inspire people, not replace their own
judgment. If someone puts another person on too high a pedestal, they may feel
disappointed the moment that person makes a mistake. The healthier approach,
I think, is to learn from a role model’s strengths without copying every detail of
their life. After all, the point is not to become someone else; it is to become a
better version of yourself.
[榜樣應該帶來啟發，而不是取代一個人本身的判斷。如果一個人把另一個人放得太高、太
神化，那麼對方一旦犯錯，他就很容易感到失望。我覺得更健康的做法，是向榜樣的優點學
習，但不要把對方人生的每一個細節都照單全收。畢竟，重點不是把自己變成另一個人，而
是讓自己成為一個更好的自己。]

components.english: For instance, someone may look up to a successful business leader because of their discipline and resilience, but that does not mean they should copy that person’s lifestyle, opinions, or every decision word for word. They can take the gold and leave the gravel.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能很欣賞某位成功商界領袖的自律和韌性，但這並不代表他就要把對方的生活方式、觀點，甚至每一個決定都原封不動地照抄。他其實可以學習真正有價值的部分，而把不適合自己的部分放下。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone may look up to a successful business leader
because of their discipline and resilience, but that does not mean they should
copy that person’s lifestyle, opinions, or every decision word for word. They can
take the gold and leave the gravel.
[例如，一個人可能很欣賞某位成功商界領袖的自律和韌性，但這並不代表他就要把對方的
生活方式、觀點，甚至每一個決定都原封不動地照抄。他其實可以學習真正有價值的部分，
而把不適合自己的部分放下。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say role models are important because they can light the way, build motivation, and encourage people to aim higher. The best ones do not live life for you; they simply help you see your own path more clearly and walk it with greater courage.
components.chinese: 總結來說，最好的榜樣不是替你活人生的人，而是那種能讓你看清方向之後，更有勇氣走自己那條路的人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say role models are important because they can light
the way, build motivation, and encourage people to aim higher. The best ones do
not live life for you; they simply help you see your own path more clearly and
walk it with greater courage.
[總結來說，最好的榜樣不是替你活人生的人，而是那種能讓你看清方向之後，更有勇氣走
自己那條路的人。]

question.english: Do you think the education system in your country influences young people’s behaviour?
question.chinese: 你認為你國家的教育制度會影響年輕人的行為嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think the education system in your country influences young
people’s behaviour?
你認為你國家的教育制度會影響年輕人的行為嗎？

components.english: Yes, absolutely. In Hong Kong, I think the education system influences young people’s behaviour quite a lot.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對會。在香港，我認為教育制度對年輕人的行為有相當大的影響。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely. In Hong Kong, I think the education system influences
young people’s behaviour quite a lot.
[是的，絕對會。在香港，我認為教育制度對年輕人的行為有相當大的影響。]

components.english: The main reason is that school does not just teach knowledge; it also teaches habits, attitudes, and ways of responding to pressure. Young people spend a huge part of their lives in that system, so it naturally leaves its mark. If a system places a lot of emphasis on exams, rankings, deadlines, and discipline, students are likely to become more cautious, more competitive, and more results-driven. In that sense, education does not simply fill the mind; it quietly shapes the person.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，學校教的不只是知識，同時也在教習慣、態度，以及面對壓力時的反應方式。年輕人在這個制度裡花了人生很大一部分時間，所以它自然會留下痕跡。如果一個制度非常強調考試、排名、截止日期，以及紀律，學生就很可能會變得更小心、更有競爭心，也更以結果為導向。從這個角度來看，教育不只是把東西裝進腦袋裡；它其實也在悄悄塑造一個人。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that school does not just teach knowledge; it
also teaches habits, attitudes, and ways of responding to pressure. Young people
spend a huge part of their lives in that system, so it naturally leaves its mark. If a
system places a lot of emphasis on exams, rankings, deadlines, and discipline,
students are likely to become more cautious, more competitive, and more
results-driven. In that sense, education does not simply fill the mind; it quietly
shapes the person.
[主要原因是，學校教的不只是知識，同時也在教習慣、態度，以及面對壓力時的反應方式。
年輕人在這個制度裡花了人生很大一部分時間，所以它自然會留下痕跡。如果一個制度非
常強調考試、排名、截止日期，以及紀律，學生就很可能會變得更小心、更有競爭心，也更以
結果為導向。從這個角度來看，教育不只是把東西裝進腦袋裡；它其實也在悄悄塑造一個

components.english: For example, in Hong Kong many students grow up being highly aware of performance, punctuality, and academic pressure. They learn to meet deadlines, follow rules, and keep going even when the workload is heavy. That can build discipline, of course, but it can also make some young people afraid of making mistakes, as if one wrong step might throw them off course.
components.chinese: 例如，在香港，很多學生從小就對成績、守時，以及學業壓力非常敏感。他們學會趕在限期前完成事情、遵守規矩，以及即使功課很重也要繼續撐下去。這當然可以培養紀律，但同時也可能令一些年輕人害怕犯錯，彷彿一步走錯就會偏離整條路。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in Hong Kong many students grow up being highly
aware of performance, punctuality, and academic pressure. They learn to meet
deadlines, follow rules, and keep going even when the workload is heavy. That
can build discipline, of course, but it can also make some young people afraid of
making mistakes, as if one wrong step might throw them off course.
[例如，在香港，很多學生從小就對成績、守時，以及學業壓力非常敏感。他們學會趕在限期
前完成事情、遵守規矩，以及即使功課很重也要繼續撐下去。這當然可以培養紀律，但同時
也可能令一些年輕人害怕犯錯，彷彿一步走錯就會偏離整條路。]

components.english: So yes, I would say the education system often leaves a clear fingerprint on the way young people behave, especially in how they work, respond to authority, and deal with pressure.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說教育制度往往會在年輕人的行為方式上留下很清晰的印記，特別是在他們如何做事、如何面對權威，以及如何處理壓力這幾方面。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say the education system often leaves a clear
fingerprint on the way young people behave, especially in how they work,
respond to authority, and deal with pressure.
[所以，是的，我會說教育制度往往會在年輕人的行為方式上留下很清晰的印記，特別是在
他們如何做事、如何面對權威，以及如何處理壓力這幾方面。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the education system is the only thing shaping young people’s behaviour.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為教育制度是唯一在塑造年輕人行為的因素。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the education system is the only thing
shaping young people’s behaviour.
[不過，我也不認為教育制度是唯一在塑造年輕人行為的因素。]

components.english: To be honest, young people are also shaped by family, friends, social media, and their own personality. A school system may set the stage, but it does not write every line of the script. Some students become more resilient and self-disciplined because of school, whereas others push back against the pressure and choose a different way of living. In other words, the system matters a great deal, but it does not act alone.
components.chinese: 老實說，年輕人同時也會受到家庭、朋友、社交媒體，以及他們本身性格的影響。學校制度也許搭好了舞台，但它並沒有寫下整份劇本。有些學生會因為學校而變得更有韌性、更自律；但也有些人會對那種壓力產生反抗，並選擇另一種生活方式。換句話說，制度當然很重要，但它並不是單獨發揮作用的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, young people are also shaped by family, friends,
social media, and their own personality. A school system may set the stage, but it
does not write every line of the script. Some students become more resilient and
self-disciplined because of school, whereas others push back against the pressure
and choose a different way of living. In other words, the system matters a great
deal, but it does not act alone.
[老實說，年輕人同時也會受到家庭、朋友、社交媒體，以及他們本身性格的影響。學校制度
也許搭好了舞台，但它並沒有寫下整份劇本。有些學生會因為學校而變得更有韌性、更自律
；但也有些人會對那種壓力產生反抗，並選擇另一種生活方式。換句話說，制度當然很重要
，但它並不是單獨發揮作用的。]

components.english: For instance, two students can go through the same education system and still turn out quite differently. One may become hardworking and focused, while the other may become anxious or rebellious. That shows the system can shape behaviour, but the final result also depends on the person and the environment around them.
components.chinese: 例如，兩個學生即使經歷同一套教育制度，最後也可能變得很不一樣。一個可能會變得勤力而專注，另一個卻可能變得焦慮，甚至有點反叛。這說明制度的確會影響行為，但最後的結果同時也取決於那個人本身，以及他身邊的環境。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, two students can go through the same education
system and still turn out quite differently. One may become hardworking and
focused, while the other may become anxious or rebellious. That shows the
system can shape behaviour, but the final result also depends on the person and
the environment around them.
[例如，兩個學生即使經歷同一套教育制度，最後也可能變得很不一樣。一個可能會變得勤
力而專注，另一個卻可能變得焦慮，甚至有點反叛。這說明制度的確會影響行為，但最後的
結果同時也取決於那個人本身，以及他身邊的環境。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the education system in Hong Kong does influence young people’s behaviour quite strongly, but it is only one thread in a much larger fabric. It can shape the rhythm of their lives, but not the whole melody of who they become.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港的教育制度的確會相當明顯地影響年輕人的行為，但它只是更大整體中的其中一條線。它可以塑造他們人生的節奏，卻不能單獨決定他們最後會成為怎樣的人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the education system in Hong Kong does
influence young people’s behaviour quite strongly, but it is only one thread in a
much larger fabric. It can shape the rhythm of their lives, but not the whole
melody of who they become.
[總結來說，我會說香港的教育制度的確會相當明顯地影響年輕人的行為，但它只是更大整
體中的其中一條線。它可以塑造他們人生的節奏，卻不能單獨決定他們最後會成為怎樣的

question.english: What type of person, such as parents, teachers, or friends, is best at influencing young people’s behaviour?
question.chinese: 主題：像父母、老師或朋友這些人之中，哪一類人最能影響年輕人的行為？

source_lines.text: Topic: What type of person, such as parents, teachers, or friends, is best at
influencing young people’s behaviour?
主題：像父母、老師或朋友這些人之中，哪一類人最能影響年輕人的行為？

components.english: If I had to choose one, I would say parents are usually the most powerful influence on young people’s behaviour, especially in the long run.
components.chinese: 如果一定要我選一類人的話，我會說父母通常是最能影響年輕人行為的人，尤其從長遠來看更是如此。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If I had to choose one, I would say parents are usually the most powerful
influence on young people’s behaviour, especially in the long run.
[如果一定要我選一類人的話，我會說父母通常是最能影響年輕人行為的人，尤其從長遠來
看更是如此。]

components.english: The main reason is that parents shape children from the very beginning. Long before young people are influenced by teachers or friends, they are already watching how their parents speak, behave, solve problems, and treat other people. In that sense, parents do not just give rules; they set the tone. They lay the foundations of a child’s character, whether that means honesty, self-discipline, kindness, or respect. Even when teenagers start pushing back, those early lessons often sink in more deeply than people realise.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，父母從一開始就已經在塑造孩子。早在年輕人受到老師或朋友影響之前，他們其實已經在觀察父母怎樣說話、怎樣做事、怎樣處理問題，以及怎樣對待別人。從這個角度來看，父母不只是訂立規矩那麼簡單；他們其實是在決定整個基調。他們為孩子的品格打下地基，無論那是誠實、自律、善良，還是尊重。即使到了青少年階段，孩子開始反抗，那些早期的教導很多時仍然比人們想像中更深地留在心裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that parents shape children from the very
beginning. Long before young people are influenced by teachers or friends, they
are already watching how their parents speak, behave, solve problems, and treat
other people. In that sense, parents do not just give rules; they set the tone. They
lay the foundations of a child’s character, whether that means honesty,
self-discipline, kindness, or respect. Even when teenagers start pushing back,
those early lessons often sink in more deeply than people realise.
[主要原因是，父母從一開始就已經在塑造孩子。早在年輕人受到老師或朋友影響之前，他
們其實已經在觀察父母怎樣說話、怎樣做事、怎樣處理問題，以及怎樣對待別人。從這個角
度來看，父母不只是訂立規矩那麼簡單；他們其實是在決定整個基調。他們為孩子的品格打
下地基，無論那是誠實、自律、善良，還是尊重。即使到了青少年階段，孩子開始反抗，那些
早期的教導很多時仍然比人們想像中更深地留在心裡。]

components.english: For example, if a child grows up in a home where the parents speak politely, keep their promises, and stay calm when problems come up, the child is quite likely to pick up those habits naturally. By contrast, if they grow up around shouting, dishonesty, or constant blame, that behaviour may rub off as well. Children often learn less from what parents say than from what parents do.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子在一個家庭裡長大，而父母平時說話有禮、守信用，並且在遇到問題時保持冷靜，那麼孩子很可能會自然地學會這些習慣。相反，如果他成長的環境充滿大喊大叫、不誠實，或者互相指責，那些行為也同樣可能沾到他身上。孩子很多時候從父母「做了甚麼」學到的，往往比從父母「說了甚麼」學到的更多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child grows up in a home where the parents speak
politely, keep their promises, and stay calm when problems come up, the child is
quite likely to pick up those habits naturally. By contrast, if they grow up
around shouting, dishonesty, or constant blame, that behaviour may rub off as
well. Children often learn less from what parents say than from what parents
do.
[例如，如果一個孩子在一個家庭裡長大，而父母平時說話有禮、守信用，並且在遇到問題
時保持冷靜，那麼孩子很可能會自然地學會這些習慣。相反，如果他成長的環境充滿大喊大
叫、不誠實，或者互相指責，那些行為也同樣可能沾到他身上。孩子很多時候從父母「做了
甚麼」學到的，往往比從父母「說了甚麼」學到的更多。]

components.english: So, overall, I would say parents are the strongest influence, because they shape the values and habits that usually sit at the heart of behaviour.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我會說父母是最強的影響力量，因為他們塑造的是價值觀和習慣，而這些東西往往正正就是行為的核心。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I would say parents are the strongest influence,
because they shape the values and habits that usually sit at the heart of
behaviour.
[所以，整體來說，我會說父母是最強的影響力量，因為他們塑造的是價值觀和習慣，而這
些東西往往正正就是行為的核心。]

components.english: That said, I do not think parents are the only important influence, because teachers and friends can be especially powerful during the teenage years.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為父母是唯一重要的影響來源，因為老師和朋友在青少年時期其實也可以有非常大的影響力。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think parents are the only important influence,
because teachers and friends can be especially powerful during the teenage years.
[不過，我也不認為父母是唯一重要的影響來源，因為老師和朋友在青少年時期其實也可以
有非常大的影響力。]

components.english: To be honest, as young people grow older, they spend more and more time outside the home, so the people around them start to shape their behaviour in very visible ways. Friends can influence the way they speak, dress, think, and even what they see as acceptable. Teachers, on the other hand, can guide students, correct them, and sometimes become a steady voice of reason. If parents lay the foundations, then teachers and friends often help shape what is built on top of them.
components.chinese: 老實說，當年輕人慢慢長大，他們會花越來越多時間在家庭以外的地方，所以身邊的人也會開始以很明顯的方式影響他們的行為。朋友可以影響他們說話的方式、穿著的風格、思考的方法，甚至連他們認為甚麼是可以接受的，也可能受朋友影響。另一方面，老師則能夠引導學生、糾正他們，有時甚至會成為一把穩定而理性的聲音。如果說父母是在打地基，那麼老師和朋友很多時就是在塑造地基之上長成甚麼樣子。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, as young people grow older, they spend more and
more time outside the home, so the people around them start to shape their
behaviour in very visible ways. Friends can influence the way they speak, dress,
think, and even what they see as acceptable. Teachers, on the other hand, can
guide students, correct them, and sometimes become a steady voice of reason. If
parents lay the foundations, then teachers and friends often help shape what is
built on top of them.
[老實說，當年輕人慢慢長大，他們會花越來越多時間在家庭以外的地方，所以身邊的人也
會開始以很明顯的方式影響他們的行為。朋友可以影響他們說話的方式、穿著的風格、思考
的方法，甚至連他們認為甚麼是可以接受的，也可能受朋友影響。另一方面，老師則能夠引
導學生、糾正他們，有時甚至會成為一把穩定而理性的聲音。如果說父母是在打地基，那麼
老師和朋友很多時就是在塑造地基之上長成甚麼樣子。]

components.english: For instance, a teenager may know from home that smoking or bullying is wrong, but if all their close friends do it, they may still feel pressure to follow the crowd. At the same time, a good teacher who notices the problem early and speaks to that student with patience and firmness may be able to pull them back before things go too far.
components.chinese: 例如，一個青少年也許從小已經知道吸煙或欺凌是不對的，但如果他身邊所有親近的朋友都在做這些事，他仍然可能會感受到壓力，想要跟著大隊走。同時，一位好的老師如果及早注意到問題，並且用耐心而堅定的方式和那位學生談談，就有可能在事情變得太嚴重之前把他拉回來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teenager may know from home that smoking or
bullying is wrong, but if all their close friends do it, they may still feel pressure
to follow the crowd. At the same time, a good teacher who notices the problem
early and speaks to that student with patience and firmness may be able to pull
them back before things go too far.
[例如，一個青少年也許從小已經知道吸煙或欺凌是不對的，但如果他身邊所有親近的朋友
都在做這些事，他仍然可能會感受到壓力，想要跟著大隊走。同時，一位好的老師如果及早
注意到問題，並且用耐心而堅定的方式和那位學生談談，就有可能在事情變得太嚴重之前
把他拉回來。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say young people in the future will be influenced above all by technology, but that influence will be filtered through family, society, and economic reality. In the end, what truly shapes them is not just what appears on the screen, but the wider world behind it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說未來年輕人最主要會受到科技影響，但這種影響仍然會經過家庭、社會，以及經濟現實的過濾。說到底，真正塑造他們的，不只是螢幕上的內容，而是螢幕背後更廣闊的整個世界。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say young people in the future will be influenced
above all by technology, but that influence will be filtered through family,
society, and economic reality. In the end, what truly shapes them is not just
what appears on the screen, but the wider world behind it.
[總結來說，我會說未來年輕人最主要會受到科技影響，但這種影響仍然會經過家庭、社會
，以及經濟現實的過濾。說到底，真正塑造他們的，不只是螢幕上的內容，而是螢幕背後更
廣闊的整個世界。]

question.english: How do you think the Internet will change people’s buying habits in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為互聯網將來會怎樣改變人們的購物習慣？

source_lines.text: Topic: How do you think the Internet will change people’s buying habits in the future?
你認為互聯網將來會怎樣改變人們的購物習慣？

components.english: I think the Internet will make people’s buying habits much faster, more personalised, and more convenience-driven in the future.
components.chinese: 我認為，未來互聯網會令人們的購物習慣變得更快、更個人化，也更以方便為主。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the Internet will make people’s buying habits much faster, more
personalised, and more convenience-driven in the future.
[我認為，未來互聯網會令人們的購物習慣變得更快、更個人化，也更以方便為主。]

components.english: The gap between wanting something and buying it is already shrinking, and I think it will shrink even more. In the past, people often had to go out, browse slowly, and think things over. In the future, they will be able to see a product online, compare prices, read reviews, and place an order almost on autopilot. In that sense, shopping may become less of a planned activity and more of a seamless reflex. The path from desire to purchase is getting shorter by the year.
components.chinese: 人們由「想要某樣東西」到「真的買下來」之間的距離，其實已經愈來愈短，而我認為未來只會更短。以前，人們很多時要親身出門、慢慢逛，再仔細想清楚才會買。但在未來，他們只要在網上看到產品、比較價格、看一下評價，幾乎就可以像自動反應一樣下單。從這個角度來看，購物可能會愈來愈不像一項特別安排好的活動，反而更像一種流暢而自然的即時反應。人們從產生慾望到完成購買的那條路，正一年比一年更短。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The gap between wanting something and buying it is already
shrinking, and I think it will shrink even more. In the past, people often had to
go out, browse slowly, and think things over. In the future, they will be able to
see a product online, compare prices, read reviews, and place an order almost on
autopilot. In that sense, shopping may become less of a planned activity and
more of a seamless reflex. The path from desire to purchase is getting shorter by
the year.
[人們由「想要某樣東西」到「真的買下來」之間的距離，其實已經愈來愈短，而我認為未來只
會更短。以前，人們很多時要親身出門、慢慢逛，再仔細想清楚才會買。但在未來，他們只要
在網上看到產品、比較價格、看一下評價，幾乎就可以像自動反應一樣下單。從這個角度來
看，購物可能會愈來愈不像一項特別安排好的活動，反而更像一種流暢而自然的即時反應。
人們從產生慾望到完成購買的那條路，正一年比一年更短。]

components.english: For example, someone might see a pair of shoes in a short video, tap a link, check a few comments, and buy them within five minutes without ever stepping into a shop. That kind of frictionless buying will probably become even more common.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能在短片裡看到一雙鞋子，按一下連結、看幾條留言，然後在五分鐘內就完成購買，甚至從頭到尾都不用走進實體店。這種幾乎沒有阻力的購物方式，未來很可能會變得更加普遍。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone might see a pair of shoes in a short video, tap
a link, check a few comments, and buy them within five minutes without ever
stepping into a shop. That kind of frictionless buying will probably become
even more common.
[例如，一個人可能在短片裡看到一雙鞋子，按一下連結、看幾條留言，然後在五分鐘內就
完成購買，甚至從頭到尾都不用走進實體店。這種幾乎沒有阻力的購物方式，未來很可能會
變得更加普遍。]

components.english: So, one major change is that people will probably shop more quickly and more casually, because the Internet keeps making the process smoother and easier.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個很大的變化就是，人們未來大概會買得更快，也更隨手，因為互聯網正不斷把整個購物過程變得更順、更方便。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major change is that people will probably shop more
quickly and more casually, because the Internet keeps making the process
smoother and easier.
[所以，其中一個很大的變化就是，人們未來大概會買得更快，也更隨手，因為互聯網正不
斷把整個購物過程變得更順、更方便。]

components.english: At the same time, I think the Internet will also make buying habits more influenced by algorithms, social media, and online opinion.
components.chinese: 與此同時，我也認為互聯網會令購物習慣更受演算法、社交媒體，以及網上輿論影響。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: At the same time, I think the Internet will also make buying habits
more influenced by algorithms, social media, and online opinion.
[與此同時，我也認為互聯網會令購物習慣更受演算法、社交媒體，以及網上輿論影響。]

components.english: In the future, people may feel that they are choosing freely, but very often their choices will be nudged by recommendations, targeted ads, influencers, and what is trending online. In other words, the Internet will not just be the marketplace; it will also be the shop assistant whispering in people’s ears. That can make shopping more efficient, but it can also make it more impulsive. People may buy things not because they truly need them, but because the product keeps popping up until it gets under their skin.
components.chinese: 在未來，人們也許會覺得自己是在自由選擇，但很多時候，他們的選擇其實會被推薦內容、精準廣告、網紅，以及網上潮流一步一步推動。換句話說，互聯網不只會是市場本身，它也會像一位一直在耳邊低聲推介的店員。這固然能令購物更有效率，但同時也可能令購物變得更衝動。人們有時買東西，未必是因為真的需要，而是因為那件產品不停出現在眼前，最後慢慢鑽進了心裡。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: In the future, people may feel that they are choosing freely, but
very often their choices will be nudged by recommendations, targeted ads,
influencers, and what is trending online. In other words, the Internet will not
just be the marketplace; it will also be the shop assistant whispering in people’s
ears. That can make shopping more efficient, but it can also make it more
impulsive. People may buy things not because they truly need them, but because
the product keeps popping up until it gets under their skin.
[在未來，人們也許會覺得自己是在自由選擇，但很多時候，他們的選擇其實會被推薦內
容、精準廣告、網紅，以及網上潮流一步一步推動。換句話說，互聯網不只會是市場本身，它
也會像一位一直在耳邊低聲推介的店員。這固然能令購物更有效率，但同時也可能令購物
變得更衝動。人們有時買東西，未必是因為真的需要，而是因為那件產品不停出現在眼前，
最後慢慢鑽進了心裡。]

components.english: For instance, a young person may open an app just to watch videos, then end up buying skincare, clothes, or gadgets because the products are presented so attractively and repeatedly. The wallet opens before the mind fully catches up.
components.chinese: 例如，一個年輕人本來可能只是打開一個應用程式看影片，結果最後卻買了護膚品、衣服，或者電子產品，因為那些商品被呈現得太吸引，而且一再重複出現。很多時候，錢包已經打開了，腦袋卻還未完全追上來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a young person may open an app just to watch videos,
then end up buying skincare, clothes, or gadgets because the products are
presented so attractively and repeatedly. The wallet opens before the mind fully
catches up.
[例如，一個年輕人本來可能只是打開一個應用程式看影片，結果最後卻買了護膚品、衣服
，或者電子產品，因為那些商品被呈現得太吸引，而且一再重複出現。很多時候，錢包已經
打開了，腦袋卻還未完全追上來。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the Internet will make future buying habits both smarter and more impulsive. People will have more information and more convenience at their fingertips, but unless they stay alert, they may also become easier to sway. So the future of shopping may be a double-edged sword: quicker, slicker, and more personalised, but not always wiser.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說互聯網將來會令人們的購物習慣同時變得更聰明，也更衝動。人們會更容易掌握資訊，也會享有更大的便利，但如果不夠清醒，他們同時也可能更容易被影響。所以，未來的購物很可能會是一把雙刃劍：更快、更順、更個人化，但未必總是更明智。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say the Internet will make future buying habits both
smarter and more impulsive. People will have more information and more
convenience at their fingertips, but unless they stay alert, they may also become
easier to sway. So the future of shopping may be a double-edged sword: quicker,
slicker, and more personalised, but not always wiser.
[總結來說，我會說互聯網將來會令人們的購物習慣同時變得更聰明，也更衝動。人們會更
容易掌握資訊，也會享有更大的便利，但如果不夠清醒，他們同時也可能更容易被影響。所
以，未來的購物很可能會是一把雙刃劍：更快、更順、更個人化，但未必總是更明智。]

question.english: What are the pros and cons of shopping online?
question.chinese: 網上購物有甚麼好處和壞處？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the pros and cons of shopping online?
網上購物有甚麼好處和壞處？

components.english: I’d say the biggest advantage of shopping online is convenience, because it puts an enormous range of products right at people’s fingertips.
components.chinese: 我會說，網上購物最大的好處就是方便，因為它把大量不同的商品直接放到人們指尖可及的地方。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say the biggest advantage of shopping online is convenience, because
it puts an enormous range of products right at people’s fingertips.
[我會說，網上購物最大的好處就是方便，因為它把大量不同的商品直接放到人們指尖可及
的地方。]

components.english: People can shop whenever they like, compare prices in minutes, read reviews, and have things delivered straight to their door. For busy people in particular, that can feel like a lifesaver. Instead of spending half a day travelling from shop to shop, they can find what they need from the comfort of home. In that sense, online shopping is a bit like carrying a whole shopping street around in your pocket.
components.chinese: 人們可以在自己想要的時間購物、幾分鐘內比較價錢、看評價，然後直接把貨品送到家門口。對特別忙碌的人來說，這種方便感有時真的像救命一樣。與其花上大半天由一間店走到另一間店，他們可以安坐家中就找到自己需要的東西。從這個角度來看，網上購物有點像把整條購物街都放進了你的口袋裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: People can shop whenever they like, compare prices in minutes,
read reviews, and have things delivered straight to their door. For busy people in
particular, that can feel like a lifesaver. Instead of spending half a day travelling
from shop to shop, they can find what they need from the comfort of home. In
that sense, online shopping is a bit like carrying a whole shopping street around
in your pocket.
[人們可以在自己想要的時間購物、幾分鐘內比較價錢、看評價，然後直接把貨品送到家門
口。對特別忙碌的人來說，這種方便感有時真的像救命一樣。與其花上大半天由一間店走到
另一間店，他們可以安坐家中就找到自己需要的東西。從這個角度來看，網上購物有點像把
整條購物街都放進了你的口袋裡。]

components.english: For example, an office worker who gets home late can still order groceries, household items, or even a birthday gift at night without rushing to a physical shop before it closes. That kind of flexibility can make daily life much easier.
components.chinese: 例如，一位很晚才下班回家的上班族，晚上仍然可以購買雜貨、日用品，甚至生日禮物，而不用趕在實體店關門前衝過去。這種彈性確實能令日常生活輕鬆得多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, an office worker who gets home late can still order
groceries, household items, or even a birthday gift at night without rushing to a
physical shop before it closes. That kind of flexibility can make daily life much
easier.
[例如，一位很晚才下班回家的上班族，晚上仍然可以購買雜貨、日用品，甚至生日禮物，而
不用趕在實體店關門前衝過去。這種彈性確實能令日常生活輕鬆得多。]

components.english: So, one clear benefit of shopping online is that it saves time, energy, and often a good deal of hassle as well.
components.chinese: 所以，網上購物其中一個很明顯的好處，就是它能節省時間、精力，而且很多時也能省掉不少麻煩。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear benefit of shopping online is that it saves time,
energy, and often a good deal of hassle as well.
[所以，網上購物其中一個很明顯的好處，就是它能節省時間、精力，而且很多時也能省掉
不少麻煩。]

components.english: That said, online shopping is also a bit of a double-edged sword, because convenience can come at the cost of certainty and self-control.
components.chinese: 不過，網上購物某程度上也是一把雙刃劍，因為方便有時是以失去確定性和自制力為代價換來的。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, online shopping is also a bit of a double-edged sword,
because convenience can come at the cost of certainty and self-control.
[不過，網上購物某程度上也是一把雙刃劍，因為方便有時是以失去確定性和自制力為代價
換來的。]

components.english: The main problem is that people cannot touch the product, try it on, or judge the quality properly before buying it. A picture can look perfect on screen and still be disappointing in real life. On top of that, shopping online can make spending feel a little too easy. With just a few taps, people can buy things they do not really need, and before they know it, they have gone down a rabbit hole of impulse purchases. So yes, it is convenient, but sometimes it is convenient in exactly the wrong way.
components.chinese: 主要問題是，人們在購買之前無法真正摸到商品、試穿，或者好好判斷質素。螢幕上的照片也許看起來完美，但實物到手時卻可能令人失望。除此之外，網上購物也會令花錢這件事變得太容易。只要按幾下，人們就可能買下一些自己其實並不需要的東西，等回過神來時，已經掉進了一堆衝動消費的兔子洞裡。所以，沒錯，它是方便，但有時偏偏是那種不太妙的方便。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The main problem is that people cannot touch the product,
try it on, or judge the quality properly before buying it. A picture can look
perfect on screen and still be disappointing in real life. On top of that, shopping
online can make spending feel a little too easy. With just a few taps, people can
buy things they do not really need, and before they know it, they have gone
down a rabbit hole of impulse purchases. So yes, it is convenient, but sometimes
it is convenient in exactly the wrong way.
[主要問題是，人們在購買之前無法真正摸到商品、試穿，或者好好判斷質素。螢幕上的照
片也許看起來完美，但實物到手時卻可能令人失望。除此之外，網上購物也會令花錢這件事
變得太容易。只要按幾下，人們就可能買下一些自己其實並不需要的東西，等回過神來時，
已經掉進了一堆衝動消費的兔子洞裡。所以，沒錯，它是方便，但有時偏偏是那種不太妙的
方便。]

components.english: For instance, someone may order a jacket online because it looks stylish in the photos, only to find that the size is wrong, the material feels cheap, and returning it is a nuisance. In the same way, people sometimes buy random items late at night simply because the checkout process is too quick and too tempting.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能因為照片裡一件外套看起來很有型就下單，結果收到後才發現尺寸不對、材質廉價，而且退貨又很麻煩。同樣地，有些人也會在深夜隨手買下一些零零碎碎的東西，只因為結帳流程實在太快，也太容易令人心動。

components.english: Overall, I’d say online shopping has more advantages than disadvantages if people use it sensibly. It is fast, flexible, and incredibly useful, but it works best when people shop with clear eyes rather than itchy fingers.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，只要人們用得理性，網上購物的好處通常還是多於壞處。它快捷、有彈性，而且非常實用，但它最適合的情況，始終是人們帶著清醒的判斷去購物，而不是手癢就亂按。

question.english: Is the Internet important for education?
question.chinese: 主題：互聯網對教育重要嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is the Internet important for education?
[主題：互聯網對教育重要嗎？]

components.english: Yes, absolutely. I think the Internet is extremely important for education today, because it has opened up learning in a way that previous generations could hardly imagine.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對重要。我認為互聯網在今天的教育中非常重要，因為它以一種過去幾代人幾乎難以想像的方式，把學習打開了。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely. I think the Internet is extremely important for
education today, because it has opened up learning in a way that previous
generations could hardly imagine.
[是的，絕對重要。我認為互聯網在今天的教育中非常重要，因為它以一種過去幾代人幾乎
難以想像的方式，把學習打開了。]

components.english: The main reason is that it gives students fast and wide access to knowledge. Instead of relying only on textbooks or whatever happens to be taught in class, learners can now watch lectures, read articles, join online courses, and look up different explanations of the same topic within minutes. In that sense, the Internet is a bit like a library without walls: it puts an enormous world of information within reach. On top of that, it allows people to learn at their own pace, which can be a game-changer for students who need more time, more practice, or a different style of explanation.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，它讓學生可以快速而廣泛地接觸知識。人們不再只依賴課本，或者只依賴課堂上剛好教到的內容；現在，學習者可以在幾分鐘之內看講座、讀文章、參加網上課程，並查找同一個題目的不同解釋。從這個角度來說，互聯網有點像一座沒有圍牆的圖書館：它把一個龐大的知識世界放到人們伸手可及的地方。除此之外，它也讓人可以按照自己的節奏學習，這對那些需要更多時間、更多練習，或者需要不同解釋方式的學生來說，往往會帶來很大的改變。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that it gives students fast and wide access to
knowledge. Instead of relying only on textbooks or whatever happens to be
taught in class, learners can now watch lectures, read articles, join online
courses, and look up different explanations of the same topic within minutes. In
that sense, the Internet is a bit like a library without walls: it puts an enormous
world of information within reach. On top of that, it allows people to learn at
their own pace, which can be a game-changer for students who need more time,
more practice, or a different style of explanation.
[主要原因是，它讓學生可以快速而廣泛地接觸知識。人們不再只依賴課本，或者只依賴課
堂上剛好教到的內容；現在，學習者可以在幾分鐘之內看講座、讀文章、參加網上課程，並
查找同一個題目的不同解釋。從這個角度來說，互聯網有點像一座沒有圍牆的圖書館：它把
一個龐大的知識世界放到人們伸手可及的地方。除此之外，它也讓人可以按照自己的節奏
學習，這對那些需要更多時間、更多練習，或者需要不同解釋方式的學生來說，往往會帶來
很大的改變。]

components.english: For example, if a student does not understand a maths concept in class, they can go online afterwards and find videos, step-by-step solutions, and extra exercises until the idea finally clicks. In the same way, someone learning a language can listen to native speakers, build vocabulary through apps, and practise with people from other countries instead of being stuck inside one classroom.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生在課堂上聽不懂某個數學概念，他之後可以上網找教學影片、一步一步的解題方法，以及額外練習，直到那個概念真正想通為止。同樣地，一個學語言的人也可以聽母語人士說話、透過應用程式累積詞彙，甚至和其他國家的人練習，而不是被困在單一課室裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a student does not understand a maths concept in
class, they can go online afterwards and find videos, step-by-step solutions, and
extra exercises until the idea finally clicks. In the same way, someone learning a
language can listen to native speakers, build vocabulary through apps, and
practise with people from other countries instead of being stuck inside one
classroom.
[例如，如果一個學生在課堂上聽不懂某個數學概念，他之後可以上網找教學影片、一步一
步的解題方法，以及額外練習，直到那個概念真正想通為止。同樣地，一個學語言的人也可
以聽母語人士說話、透過應用程式累積詞彙，甚至和其他國家的人練習，而不是被困在單一
課室裡。]

components.english: So yes, I would definitely say the Internet is important for education, because it makes learning broader, faster, and far more flexible.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我一定會說互聯網對教育很重要，因為它讓學習變得更廣闊、更快速，也更有彈性。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would definitely say the Internet is important for
education, because it makes learning broader, faster, and far more flexible.
[所以，是的，我一定會說互聯網對教育很重要，因為它讓學習變得更廣闊、更快速，也更有
彈性。]

components.english: That said, I would not say the Internet alone can guarantee a good education, because it is a tool, not a teacher in itself.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會說單靠互聯網就能保證良好的教育，因為它始終是一種工具，而不是老師本身。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not say the Internet alone can guarantee a good
education, because it is a tool, not a teacher in itself.
[不過，我也不會說單靠互聯網就能保證良好的教育，因為它始終是一種工具，而不是老師
本身。]

components.english: The problem is that the Internet is a double-edged sword. It offers knowledge, but it also offers distraction, misinformation, and endless noise. If students are not guided properly, they can easily mistake quick answers for real understanding, or spend more time scrolling than studying. A screen can open doors, but it cannot replace discipline, critical thinking, or human guidance. So while the Internet can turbocharge education, it works best when it goes hand in hand with good teaching and good habits.
components.chinese: 問題在於，互聯網本身是一把雙刃劍。它能提供知識，但同時也帶來分心、錯誤資訊，以及無窮無盡的雜音。如果學生沒有得到適當引導，他們很容易把「快速找到答案」誤以為是真正理解，或者花在滑動內容上的時間比真正讀書還多。螢幕可以打開很多門，但它無法取代自律、批判思考，以及真人引導。所以，雖然互聯網可以大大提升教育效果，但它通常是在配合良好教學和良好習慣時，才最能發揮作用。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The problem is that the Internet is a double-edged sword. It
offers knowledge, but it also offers distraction, misinformation, and endless
noise. If students are not guided properly, they can easily mistake quick answers
for real understanding, or spend more time scrolling than studying. A screen
can open doors, but it cannot replace discipline, critical thinking, or human
guidance. So while the Internet can turbocharge education, it works best when
it goes hand in hand with good teaching and good habits.
[問題在於，互聯網本身是一把雙刃劍。它能提供知識，但同時也帶來分心、錯誤資訊，以及
無窮無盡的雜音。如果學生沒有得到適當引導，他們很容易把「快速找到答案」誤以為是真
正理解，或者花在滑動內容上的時間比真正讀書還多。螢幕可以打開很多門，但它無法取代
自律、批判思考，以及真人引導。所以，雖然互聯網可以大大提升教育效果，但它通常是在
配合良好教學和良好習慣時，才最能發揮作用。]

components.english: For instance, a student may use the Internet to research an essay, which is helpful, but if they simply copy the first thing they find without questioning it, the learning stays shallow. By contrast, if a teacher helps them evaluate sources and think independently, the same Internet becomes a much more powerful learning tool.
components.chinese: 例如，一個學生可能會利用互聯網去搜集寫文章的資料，這本來很有幫助；但如果他只是把找到的第一份內容照抄，而沒有加以判斷，那麼學習就會停留在很表面的層次。相反，如果老師能幫助他評估資料來源，並訓練他獨立思考，那麼同一個互聯網就會變成更有力量的學習工具。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a student may use the Internet to research an essay,
which is helpful, but if they simply copy the first thing they find without
questioning it, the learning stays shallow. By contrast, if a teacher helps them
evaluate sources and think independently, the same Internet becomes a much
more powerful learning tool.
[例如，一個學生可能會利用互聯網去搜集寫文章的資料，這本來很有幫助；但如果他只是
把找到的第一份內容照抄，而沒有加以判斷，那麼學習就會停留在很表面的層次。相反，如
果老師能幫助他評估資料來源，並訓練他獨立思考，那麼同一個互聯網就會變成更有力量
的學習工具。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the Internet is essential for modern education, but its real value depends on how wisely it is used. At its best, it is a bridge to knowledge; at its worst, it can be little more than a sea of noise.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說互聯網對現代教育是不可或缺的，但它真正的價值取決於人們是否懂得明智地使用它。它在最好時像一座通往知識的橋樑，但在最差時也可能只是一片資訊噪音。
