明智地使用它。它在最好時像一座通往知識的橋樑，但在最差時也可能只是一片資訊噪

question.english: Do you think parents should supervise their children’s use of the Internet?
question.chinese: 你認為父母應該監管孩子使用網絡嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think parents should supervise their children’s use of the Internet?
你認為父母應該監管孩子使用網絡嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do, especially when children are still young, because the Internet can be incredibly useful but also quite risky.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為應該，尤其是在孩子年紀還小的時候，因為互聯網雖然非常有用，但同時也可能帶來不少風險。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do, especially when children are still young, because the Internet
can be incredibly useful but also quite risky.
[是的，我認為應該，尤其是在孩子年紀還小的時候，因為互聯網雖然非常有用，但同時也
可能帶來不少風險。]

components.english: The main reason is that children do not always know how to tell the difference between what is helpful and what is harmful. Online, they can come across inappropriate content, misinformation, scams, or even strangers with bad intentions. In that sense, the Internet can be a gold mine, but it can also be a minefield. So I think parental supervision is not about invading a child’s world; it is more about putting up guardrails while they are still learning the road.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，孩子未必總是懂得分辨甚麼是有幫助的，甚麼是有害的。在網上，他們可能會接觸到不適當內容、錯誤資訊、騙局，甚至是別有用心的陌生人。從這個角度來說，互聯網可以是一座金礦，但同時也可能像一片地雷區。所以我覺得父母的監管不是為了闖進孩子的世界，而更像是在他們還未學會走路之前，先在路邊設好護欄。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that children do not always know how to tell
the difference between what is helpful and what is harmful. Online, they can
come across inappropriate content, misinformation, scams, or even strangers
with bad intentions. In that sense, the Internet can be a gold mine, but it can
also be a minefield. So I think parental supervision is not about invading a
child’s world; it is more about putting up guardrails while they are still learning
the road.
[主要原因是，孩子未必總是懂得分辨甚麼是有幫助的，甚麼是有害的。在網上，他們可能
會接觸到不適當內容、錯誤資訊、騙局，甚至是別有用心的陌生人。從這個角度來說，互聯
網可以是一座金礦，但同時也可能像一片地雷區。所以我覺得父母的監管不是為了闖進孩
子的世界，而更像是在他們還未學會走路之前，先在路邊設好護欄。]

components.english: For example, a child may go online simply to watch cartoons or do homework, but after a few clicks, they might end up on content that is violent, misleading, or completely unsuitable for their age. Without any guidance, it is easy for them to wander into places they are not ready for.
components.chinese: 例如，一個孩子本來可能只是上網看卡通片，或者做功課，但點幾下之後，就可能去到一些充滿暴力、誤導性很強，或者根本不適合他們年齡的內容。如果沒有任何引導，他們很容易就會走進一些自己還未準備好面對的地方。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a child may go online simply to watch cartoons or do
homework, but after a few clicks, they might end up on content that is violent,
misleading, or completely unsuitable for their age. Without any guidance, it is
easy for them to wander into places they are not ready for.
[例如，一個孩子本來可能只是上網看卡通片，或者做功課，但點幾下之後，就可能去到一
些充滿暴力、誤導性很強，或者根本不適合他們年齡的內容。如果沒有任何引導，他們很容
易就會走進一些自己還未準備好面對的地方。]

components.english: So yes, I would say parents should supervise their children’s Internet use, because children need protection as well as freedom.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說父母應該監管孩子使用網絡，因為孩子需要的，不只是自由，也包括保護。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say parents should supervise their children’s
Internet use, because children need protection as well as freedom.
[所以，是的，我會說父母應該監管孩子使用網絡，因為孩子需要的，不只是自由，也包括保

components.english: That said, I do not think supervision should mean controlling every click, because that can backfire as children grow older.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為監管就等於要控制每一次點擊，因為當孩子慢慢長大，這種做法反而可能適得其反。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think supervision should mean controlling every
click, because that can backfire as children grow older.
[不過，我也不認為監管就等於要控制每一次點擊，因為當孩子慢慢長大，這種做法反而可
能適得其反。]

components.english: If parents are too strict, children may simply hide what they are doing instead of learning how to use the Internet wisely. In my view, the goal should be to teach judgment, not just impose rules. After all, parents cannot look over their children’s shoulder forever. At some point, children need to understand privacy, online safety, screen-time balance, and how to think critically for themselves.
components.chinese: 如果父母管得太緊，孩子很可能只會把自己在做的事情藏起來，而不是學會怎樣更明智地使用網絡。在我看來，真正的目標應該是教會孩子判斷力，而不只是訂下一堆規矩。畢竟，父母不可能一輩子都站在孩子背後盯著他們看。到了某個階段，孩子需要自己明白甚麼是私隱、甚麼是網絡安全、怎樣平衡螢幕時間，以及怎樣獨立思考。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If parents are too strict, children may simply hide what they
are doing instead of learning how to use the Internet wisely. In my view, the
goal should be to teach judgment, not just impose rules. After all, parents
cannot look over their children’s shoulder forever. At some point, children need
to understand privacy, online safety, screen-time balance, and how to think
critically for themselves.
[如果父母管得太緊，孩子很可能只會把自己在做的事情藏起來，而不是學會怎樣更明智地
使用網絡。在我看來，真正的目標應該是教會孩子判斷力，而不只是訂下一堆規矩。畢竟，
父母不可能一輩子都站在孩子背後盯著他們看。到了某個階段，孩子需要自己明白甚麼是
私隱、甚麼是網絡安全、怎樣平衡螢幕時間，以及怎樣獨立思考。]

components.english: For instance, with a teenager, it may be better for parents to talk openly about fake news, cyberbullying, and online privacy than to check every message in secret. That way, the child learns not only what to avoid, but also why it matters.
components.chinese: 例如，面對一個青少年，父母與其偷偷查看每一則訊息，不如坦白地和他談談假新聞、網絡欺凌，以及網上私隱。那樣一來，孩子學到的就不只是甚麼不應該做，還會明白背後真正重要的原因。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, with a teenager, it may be better for parents to talk
openly about fake news, cyberbullying, and online privacy than to check every
message in secret. That way, the child learns not only what to avoid, but also
why it matters.
[例如，面對一個青少年，父母與其偷偷查看每一則訊息，不如坦白地和他談談假新聞、網
絡欺凌，以及網上私隱。那樣一來，孩子學到的就不只是甚麼不應該做，還會明白背後真正
重要的原因。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say parents should definitely supervise their children’s use of the Internet, but in a balanced way. A steady hand is better than a heavy hand: children need guidance, protection, and, little by little, the wisdom to stand on their own online.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說父母的確應該監管孩子使用網絡，但方式應該是平衡的。穩穩地引導，會比重重地壓住更好：孩子需要的是指引、保護，以及一步一步學會在網絡世界裡自己站穩的能力。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say parents should definitely supervise their
children’s use of the Internet, but in a balanced way. A steady hand is better
than a heavy hand: children need guidance, protection, and, little by little, the
wisdom to stand on their own online.
[總結來說，我會說父母的確應該監管孩子使用網絡，但方式應該是平衡的。穩穩地引導，
會比重重地壓住更好：孩子需要的是指引、保護，以及一步一步學會在網絡世界裡自己站穩
的能力。]

question.english: What’s the best age for children to use the Internet?
question.chinese: 你認為孩子幾歲開始使用互聯網是最合適的？

source_lines.text: Topic: What’s the best age for children to use the Internet?
你認為孩子幾歲開始使用互聯網是最合適的？

components.english: Personally, I think the best age is probably around ten to twelve, provided that children use it with clear guidance and sensible limits.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為最合適的年齡大概是十至十二歲左右，前提是孩子在使用時有清晰的引導和合理的限制。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think the best age is probably around ten to twelve,
provided that children use it with clear guidance and sensible limits.
[就我個人而言，我認為最合適的年齡大概是十至十二歲左右，前提是孩子在使用時有清晰
的引導和合理的限制。]

components.english: The main reason is that by that age, many children are usually mature enough to understand basic rules, online safety, and the difference between using the Internet as a tool and getting lost in it. The Internet can open doors to learning, creativity, and communication, but it can also be a bit like a huge city: useful and exciting, yet not somewhere a child should wander through without a map. So I think late primary school is often a sensible middle ground.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，到了那個年紀，很多孩子通常已經成熟到能明白基本規則、網上安全，以及把互聯網當工具使用和沉迷其中之間的分別。互聯網可以為學習、創意和溝通打開很多門，但它同時也有點像一座巨大的城市：既有用又吸引人，但孩子不應該在沒有地圖的情況下隨便亂走。所以我認為，小學高年級往往是一個比較合理的平衡點。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that by that age, many children are usually
mature enough to understand basic rules, online safety, and the difference
between using the Internet as a tool and getting lost in it. The Internet can
open doors to learning, creativity, and communication, but it can also be a bit
like a huge city: useful and exciting, yet not somewhere a child should wander
through without a map. So I think late primary school is often a sensible middle
ground.
[主要原因是，到了那個年紀，很多孩子通常已經成熟到能明白基本規則、網上安全，以及
把互聯網當工具使用和沉迷其中之間的分別。互聯網可以為學習、創意和溝通打開很多門，
但它同時也有點像一座巨大的城市：既有用又吸引人，但孩子不應該在沒有地圖的情況下
隨便亂走。所以我認為，小學高年級往往是一個比較合理的平衡點。]

components.english: For example, a child of eleven may use the Internet to do homework, watch educational videos, look up information, or message classmates about schoolwork. At that stage, the Internet can genuinely support learning, as long as parents or teachers still keep an eye on how it is being used.
components.chinese: 例如，一個十一歲的孩子可能會用互聯網做功課、看教學影片、搜尋資料，或者和同學聯絡課業上的事情。在這個階段，只要父母或老師仍然留意他怎樣使用，互聯網其實可以真真正正地幫助學習。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a child of eleven may use the Internet to do homework,
watch educational videos, look up information, or message classmates about
schoolwork. At that stage, the Internet can genuinely support learning, as long
as parents or teachers still keep an eye on how it is being used.
[例如，一個十一歲的孩子可能會用互聯網做功課、看教學影片、搜尋資料，或者和同學聯
絡課業上的事情。在這個階段，只要父母或老師仍然留意他怎樣使用，互聯網其實可以真真
正正地幫助學習。]

components.english: So, if I had to choose an age, I’d say around ten to twelve is a good time, because children are old enough to benefit from it, but still young enough to need guidance.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要選一個年齡，我會說十至十二歲左右是一個不錯的時間，因為孩子已經大到可以從中受益，但同時又仍然年輕到需要引導。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to choose an age, I’d say around ten to twelve is a
good time, because children are old enough to benefit from it, but still young
enough to need guidance.
[所以，如果一定要選一個年齡，我會說十至十二歲左右是一個不錯的時間，因為孩子已經
大到可以從中受益，但同時又仍然年輕到需要引導。]

components.english: That said, I do not think there is one perfect age that fits every child, because children mature at different speeds.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為有一個放諸四海皆準的完美年齡，因為每個孩子成熟的速度都不一樣。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think there is one perfect age that fits every child,
because children mature at different speeds.
[不過，我並不認為有一個放諸四海皆準的完美年齡，因為每個孩子成熟的速度都不一樣。]

components.english: To be honest, age matters, but judgment matters more. Some younger children may be ready for limited, supervised use, whereas some older ones may still struggle with self-control, screen time, or online risks. In other words, the real question is not just “How old are they?” but also “How ready are they?” The best approach, in my view, is to introduce the Internet gradually rather than throw children in at the deep end.
components.chinese: 老實說，年齡固然重要，但判斷能力更重要。有些年紀較小的孩子，也許已經準備好在有限和受監督的情況下使用；相反，有些年紀較大的孩子，卻仍然可能在自制力、螢幕時間，或者網上風險方面出現問題。換句話說，真正的問題不只是「他們幾歲」，而是「他們準備好了沒有」。在我看來，最好的做法是循序漸進地讓孩子接觸互聯網，而不是一下子把他們丟進深水區。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, age matters, but judgment matters more. Some
younger children may be ready for limited, supervised use, whereas some older
ones may still struggle with self-control, screen time, or online risks. In other
words, the real question is not just “How old are they?” but also “How ready are
they?” The best approach, in my view, is to introduce the Internet gradually
rather than throw children in at the deep end.
[老實說，年齡固然重要，但判斷能力更重要。有些年紀較小的孩子，也許已經準備好在有
限和受監督的情況下使用；相反，有些年紀較大的孩子，卻仍然可能在自制力、螢幕時間，
或者網上風險方面出現問題。換句話說，真正的問題不只是「他們幾歲」，而是「他們準備好
了沒有」。在我看來，最好的做法是循序漸進地讓孩子接觸互聯網，而不是一下子把他們丟
進深水區。]

components.english: For instance, an eight-year-old might use the Internet with a parent beside them to watch something educational, while a thirteen-year-old may be allowed more independence but still need rules about privacy, apps, and screen time. So the level of freedom should grow step by step, not all at once.
components.chinese: 例如，一個八歲小朋友可能會在父母陪同下上網看一些有教育意義的內容；而一個十三歲的孩子，雖然可以有更多自主權，但仍然需要在私隱、應用程式和使用時間方面有明確規則。所以，自由度應該是一步一步增加，而不是一下子完全放手。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, an eight-year-old might use the Internet with a parent
beside them to watch something educational, while a thirteen-year-old may be
allowed more independence but still need rules about privacy, apps, and screen
time. So the level of freedom should grow step by step, not all at once.
[例如，一個八歲小朋友可能會在父母陪同下上網看一些有教育意義的內容；而一個十三歲
的孩子，雖然可以有更多自主權，但仍然需要在私隱、應用程式和使用時間方面有明確規
則。所以，自由度應該是一步一步增加，而不是一下子完全放手。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the best age for children to use the Internet is around late primary school, but it depends less on a magic number than on maturity, supervision, and purpose. The Internet can be a brilliant tool in the right hands, but children need to grow into it, not just be handed it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說孩子最適合開始使用互聯網的年齡，大概是在小學高年級，但真正關鍵的與其說是一個神奇數字，不如說是成熟程度、監督方式，以及使用目的。互聯網在合適的人手上可以是很出色的工具，但孩子需要慢慢長進到能駕馭它，而不是只是被直接交到手上。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the best age for children to use the Internet is
around late primary school, but it depends less on a magic number than on
maturity, supervision, and purpose. The Internet can be a brilliant tool in the
right hands, but children need to grow into it, not just be handed it.
[總結來說，我會說孩子最適合開始使用互聯網的年齡，大概是在小學高年級，但真正關鍵
的與其說是一個神奇數字，不如說是成熟程度、監督方式，以及使用目的。互聯網在合適的
人手上可以是很出色的工具，但孩子需要慢慢長進到能駕馭它，而不是只是被直接交到手

question.english: Why do children start using the Internet very early nowadays?
question.chinese: 主題：為甚麼現在的孩子很早就開始使用互聯網？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do children start using the Internet very early nowadays?
主題：為甚麼現在的孩子很早就開始使用互聯網？

components.english: I think children start using the Internet very early nowadays mainly because they are growing up in a world where being online is almost part of everyday life.
components.chinese: 我認為，現在的孩子之所以很早就開始使用互聯網，主要是因為他們成長的世界裡，「上網」幾乎已經成了日常生活的一部分。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think children start using the Internet very early nowadays mainly
because they are growing up in a world where being online is almost part of
everyday life.
[我認為，現在的孩子之所以很早就開始使用互聯網，主要是因為他們成長的世界裡，「上
網」幾乎已經成了日常生活的一部分。]

components.english: The main reason is that the Internet is now woven into so many parts of life, from entertainment and communication to education and daily routines. Children do not see it as some special piece of technology; to them, it is just there, like electricity or television used to be. In that sense, they are born into a wired world. So, instead of stepping into the Internet later in life, they are surrounded by it from the very beginning.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，互聯網現在已經深深融入生活的很多部分，從娛樂、溝通，到教育和日常習慣都離不開它。孩子並不會把它看成甚麼特別高科技的東西；對他們來說，它就只是一直都在，某程度上就像以前的電力或電視一樣自然。從這個角度來說，他們其實是出生在一個高度連線的世界裡。所以，他們並不是長大後才慢慢走進互聯網，而是一開始就已經被它包圍。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that the Internet is now woven into so many
parts of life, from entertainment and communication to education and daily
routines. Children do not see it as some special piece of technology; to them, it is
just there, like electricity or television used to be. In that sense, they are born
into a wired world. So, instead of stepping into the Internet later in life, they
are surrounded by it from the very beginning.
[主要原因是，互聯網現在已經深深融入生活的很多部分，從娛樂、溝通，到教育和日常習
慣都離不開它。孩子並不會把它看成甚麼特別高科技的東西；對他們來說，它就只是一直都
在，某程度上就像以前的電力或電視一樣自然。從這個角度來說，他們其實是出生在一個高
度連線的世界裡。所以，他們並不是長大後才慢慢走進互聯網，而是一開始就已經被它包

components.english: For example, even very young children may watch nursery rhymes online, play simple learning games on a tablet, or video-call relatives who live far away. By the time they reach primary school, many of them are already using the Internet for homework, educational apps, or classroom platforms. Before long, going online feels as natural to them as turning on the TV once felt to older generations.
components.chinese: 例如，即使是很年幼的孩子，也可能已經會在網上看兒歌、用平板玩一些簡單的學習遊戲，或者和住得很遠的親戚視像通話。等到他們上小學時，很多人其實已經開始用互聯網做功課、使用學習應用程式，或者登入學校平台。沒過多久，對他們來說，上網就會變得像上一代人打開電視一樣自然。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, even very young children may watch nursery rhymes
online, play simple learning games on a tablet, or video-call relatives who live far
away. By the time they reach primary school, many of them are already using the
Internet for homework, educational apps, or classroom platforms. Before long,
going online feels as natural to them as turning on the TV once felt to older
generations.
[例如，即使是很年幼的孩子，也可能已經會在網上看兒歌、用平板玩一些簡單的學習遊戲
，或者和住得很遠的親戚視像通話。等到他們上小學時，很多人其實已經開始用互聯網做功
課、使用學習應用程式，或者登入學校平台。沒過多久，對他們來說，上網就會變得像上一
代人打開電視一樣自然。]

components.english: So, I would say children start early not because they are unusually tech-minded, but because the Internet has become part of the background of modern childhood.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說，孩子這麼早開始上網，並不是因為他們特別偏向科技，而是因為互聯網本身已經成了現代童年背景的一部分。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I would say children start early not because they are
unusually tech-minded, but because the Internet has become part of the
background of modern childhood.
[所以，我會說，孩子這麼早開始上網，並不是因為他們特別偏向科技，而是因為互聯網本
身已經成了現代童年背景的一部分。]

components.english: That said, another important reason is that adults often see the Internet as useful, convenient, and sometimes almost impossible to avoid.
components.chinese: 不過，另一個重要原因是，大人很多時都覺得互聯網既有用、又方便，而且有時幾乎很難完全避開。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, another important reason is that adults often see the
Internet as useful, convenient, and sometimes almost impossible to avoid.
[不過，另一個重要原因是，大人很多時都覺得互聯網既有用、又方便，而且有時幾乎很難
完全避開。]

components.english: To be honest, parents and teachers often introduce children to the Internet for practical reasons. Parents may use it to keep children occupied, calm, or entertained, while teachers may use it for videos, research tasks, or online assignments. In other words, children are not always the ones driving this early exposure; often, adults are opening the door for them. Of course, that can be helpful, but it is also a bit of a double-edged sword, because early access can educate children, yet at the same time expose them to distractions or risks if there is no guidance.
components.chinese: 老實說，家長和老師很多時讓孩子接觸互聯網，都是出於很實際的原因。家長可能會用它來讓孩子安靜下來、分散注意力，或者打發時間；老師則可能會用它來播放影片、安排資料搜集，或者派發網上功課。換句話說，孩子未必永遠是主動要求提早接觸網絡的一方；很多時候，其實是大人先替他們把門打開。當然，這樣做可以有幫助，但同時也有點像一把雙刃劍，因為早期接觸既可以帶來學習機會，也可能在缺乏引導的情況下，讓孩子暴露於分心或風險之中。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, parents and teachers often introduce children to
the Internet for practical reasons. Parents may use it to keep children occupied,
calm, or entertained, while teachers may use it for videos, research tasks, or
online assignments. In other words, children are not always the ones driving
this early exposure; often, adults are opening the door for them. Of course, that
can be helpful, but it is also a bit of a double-edged sword, because early access
can educate children, yet at the same time expose them to distractions or risks if
there is no guidance.
[老實說，家長和老師很多時讓孩子接觸互聯網，都是出於很實際的原因。家長可能會用它
來讓孩子安靜下來、分散注意力，或者打發時間；老師則可能會用它來播放影片、安排資料
搜集，或者派發網上功課。換句話說，孩子未必永遠是主動要求提早接觸網絡的一方；很多
時候，其實是大人先替他們把門打開。當然，這樣做可以有幫助，但同時也有點像一把雙刃
劍，因為早期接觸既可以帶來學習機會，也可能在缺乏引導的情況下，讓孩子暴露於分心或
風險之中。]

components.english: For instance, a busy parent may hand a child a phone to watch cartoons while they finish some work, and a school may later ask that same child to use the Internet for a simple project. Step by step, screen use becomes normal long before the child is mature enough to understand everything that comes with it.
components.chinese: 例如，一位忙碌的家長可能會把手機交給孩子看卡通，好讓自己先完成手上的事情；而學校之後又可能會要求那個孩子上網做一個簡單的小項目。就這樣一步一步，孩子對屏幕和網絡的使用便會變得十分平常，甚至早於他真正有足夠成熟度去理解當中所有影響。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a busy parent may hand a child a phone to watch
cartoons while they finish some work, and a school may later ask that same child
to use the Internet for a simple project. Step by step, screen use becomes normal
long before the child is mature enough to understand everything that comes
with it.
[例如，一位忙碌的家長可能會把手機交給孩子看卡通，好讓自己先完成手上的事情；而學
校之後又可能會要求那個孩子上網做一個簡單的小項目。就這樣一步一步，孩子對屏幕和
網絡的使用便會變得十分平常，甚至早於他真正有足夠成熟度去理解當中所有影響。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say children start using the Internet very early nowadays because it is deeply woven into entertainment, education, and family life. The real question is not whether they should touch it early, but whether they are taught to use it wisely, safely, and with proper limits.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說現在的孩子之所以很早就開始使用互聯網，是因為它已經深深融入娛樂、教育，以及家庭生活之中。真正的問題其實不是他們是否太早接觸它，而是他們有沒有被好好教導，懂得怎樣更明智、更安全、也更有節制地使用它。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say children start using the Internet very early
nowadays because it is deeply woven into entertainment, education, and family
life. The real question is not whether they should touch it early, but whether
they are taught to use it wisely, safely, and with proper limits.
[總結來說，我會說現在的孩子之所以很早就開始使用互聯網，是因為它已經深深融入娛
樂、教育，以及家庭生活之中。真正的問題其實不是他們是否太早接觸它，而是他們有沒有
被好好教導，懂得怎樣更明智、更安全、也更有節制地使用它。]

question.english: Let’s move on to what people do on the Internet. What do you think people do on the Internet?
question.chinese: 我們來談談人們在互聯網上做甚麼。你認為人們會在網上做些甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: Let’s move on to what people do on the Internet. What do you think
people do on the Internet?
我們來談談人們在互聯網上做甚麼。你認為人們會在網上做些甚麼？

components.english: I think most people go on the Internet mainly to communicate, find information, and entertain themselves, because it has become woven into the fabric of everyday life.
components.chinese: 我認為，大多數人上網主要是為了溝通、尋找資訊，以及娛樂自己，因為互聯網已經深深融入日常生活之中。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think most people go on the Internet mainly to communicate, find
information, and entertain themselves, because it has become woven into the
fabric of everyday life.
[我認為，大多數人上網主要是為了溝通、尋找資訊，以及娛樂自己，因為互聯網已經深深
融入日常生活之中。]

components.english: These days, the Internet is no longer just a tool people use now and then; it is more like a space people live in for part of the day. They go online to chat with friends, scroll through social media, watch videos, read the news, look things up, or simply kill time. In that sense, the Internet is a bit like a giant digital high street: people go there to browse, connect, and see what is going on.
components.chinese: 如今，互聯網已經不再只是人們偶爾使用的一種工具；它更像是一個人們每天都會待上一段時間的空間。人們會上網和朋友聊天、滑社交媒體、看影片、讀新聞、查資料，或者單純打發時間。從這個角度來說，互聯網有點像一條巨大的數碼商業街：人們會去那裡瀏覽、聯繫他人，並看看外面的世界正在發生甚麼事。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: These days, the Internet is no longer just a tool people use now
and then; it is more like a space people live in for part of the day. They go online
to chat with friends, scroll through social media, watch videos, read the news,
look things up, or simply kill time. In that sense, the Internet is a bit like a giant
digital high street: people go there to browse, connect, and see what is going on.
[如今，互聯網已經不再只是人們偶爾使用的一種工具；它更像是一個人們每天都會待上一
段時間的空間。人們會上網和朋友聊天、滑社交媒體、看影片、讀新聞、查資料，或者單純打
發時間。從這個角度來說，互聯網有點像一條巨大的數碼商業街：人們會去那裡瀏覽、聯繫
他人，並看看外面的世界正在發生甚麼事。]

components.english: For example, a student might wake up and check messages, watch a short video while having breakfast, search for information for a school assignment, and then listen to music online later in the day. Even before they realise it, the Internet has already shaped a large part of their routine.
components.chinese: 例如，一位學生可能一早起床就先看訊息，吃早餐時順便看一段短片，之後為學校功課上網找資料，到了晚一點又在網上聽音樂。很多時候，甚至在他自己還沒意識到之前，互聯網其實已經影響了他生活作息中的很大一部分。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a student might wake up and check messages, watch a
short video while having breakfast, search for information for a school
assignment, and then listen to music online later in the day. Even before they
realise it, the Internet has already shaped a large part of their routine.
[例如，一位學生可能一早起床就先看訊息，吃早餐時順便看一段短片，之後為學校功課上
網找資料，到了晚一點又在網上聽音樂。很多時候，甚至在他自己還沒意識到之前，互聯網
其實已經影響了他生活作息中的很大一部分。]

components.english: So, broadly speaking, I would say people use the Internet to stay connected, stay informed, and switch off for a while when they need a break.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我會說人們使用互聯網，是為了與世界保持聯繫、掌握資訊，以及在需要休息的時候讓自己暫時放鬆一下。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, broadly speaking, I would say people use the Internet to stay
connected, stay informed, and switch off for a while when they need a break.
[所以，整體來說，我會說人們使用互聯網，是為了與世界保持聯繫、掌握資訊，以及在需要
休息的時候讓自己暫時放鬆一下。]

components.english: That said, people do not just use the Internet for fun; they also rely on it for practical and essential parts of life.
components.chinese: 不過，人們使用互聯網並不只是為了消遣；他們也很依賴它去處理生活中一些實際而且重要的事情。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, people do not just use the Internet for fun; they also rely on
it for practical and essential parts of life.
[不過，人們使用互聯網並不只是為了消遣；他們也很依賴它去處理生活中一些實際而且重
要的事情。]

components.english: A lot of people now use the Internet to work, study, shop, pay bills, book tickets, and manage daily responsibilities. In other words, it is not only a place for entertainment, but also a tool for getting things done. You could even say it is part office, part classroom, and part marketplace all rolled into one. That is why for many people, being offline for too long feels as if a part of daily life has suddenly gone missing.
components.chinese: 現在很多人都會利用互聯網去工作、學習、購物、繳費、訂票，以及處理日常責任。換句話說，它不單是一個娛樂場所，同時也是一個幫助人完成事情的工具。你甚至可以說，它就像把辦公室、課室和商場都合而為一。正因如此，對很多人來說，如果太久不能上網，往往就會有一種生活忽然缺了一塊的感覺。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A lot of people now use the Internet to work, study, shop, pay
bills, book tickets, and manage daily responsibilities. In other words, it is not
only a place for entertainment, but also a tool for getting things done. You could
even say it is part office, part classroom, and part marketplace all rolled into one.
That is why for many people, being offline for too long feels as if a part of daily
life has suddenly gone missing.
[現在很多人都會利用互聯網去工作、學習、購物、繳費、訂票，以及處理日常責任。換句話
說，它不單是一個娛樂場所，同時也是一個幫助人完成事情的工具。你甚至可以說，它就像
把辦公室、課室和商場都合而為一。正因如此，對很多人來說，如果太久不能上網，往往就
會有一種生活忽然缺了一塊的感覺。]

components.english: For instance, an office worker may attend an online meeting in the morning, order groceries at lunchtime, transfer money through a banking app in the afternoon, and take an online course in the evening. That shows the Internet is not just where people pass the time; it is where they organise their lives.
components.chinese: 例如，一位上班族可能早上參加網上會議，中午用手機買日用品，下午透過銀行應用程式轉帳，晚上再上網修讀課程。這就顯示出，互聯網不只是人們消磨時間的地方，更是他們安排生活、處理事情的地方。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, an office worker may attend an online meeting in the
morning, order groceries at lunchtime, transfer money through a banking app
in the afternoon, and take an online course in the evening. That shows the
Internet is not just where people pass the time; it is where they organise their
lives.
[例如，一位上班族可能早上參加網上會議，中午用手機買日用品，下午透過銀行應用程式
轉帳，晚上再上網修讀課程。這就顯示出，互聯網不只是人們消磨時間的地方，更是他們安
排生活、處理事情的地方。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people use the Internet for almost everything, from relaxation to responsibility. It is no longer just somewhere people visit; for many, it has become part of how they live, work, and think.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們幾乎甚麼都會在網上做，從放鬆消遣到承擔責任都包括在內。它已經不再只是一個人們「去」的地方；對很多人來說，它已經變成了他們生活、工作和思考方式的一部分。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people use the Internet for almost everything,
from relaxation to responsibility. It is no longer just somewhere people visit; for
many, it has become part of how they live, work, and think.
[總結來說，我會說人們幾乎甚麼都會在網上做，從放鬆消遣到承擔責任都包括在內。它已
經不再只是一個人們「去」的地方；對很多人來說，它已經變成了他們生活、工作和思考方式
的一部分。]

question.english: What can people do on social media?
question.chinese: 人們可以在社交媒體上做些甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: What can people do on social media?
人們可以在社交媒體上做些甚麼？

components.english: People can do a huge range of things on social media, but I’d say the most common ones are staying connected, sharing content, and keeping up with what is happening around them.
components.chinese: 人們在社交媒體上其實可以做很多事情，不過我會說，最常見的主要是保持聯繫、分享內容，以及追蹤周圍正在發生的事。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: People can do a huge range of things on social media, but I’d say the
most common ones are staying connected, sharing content, and keeping up with
what is happening around them.
[人們在社交媒體上其實可以做很多事情，不過我會說，最常見的主要是保持聯繫、分享內
容，以及追蹤周圍正在發生的事。]

components.english: The reason is quite simple: social media has become a kind of digital meeting place. People use it to talk to friends, post photos, react to other people’s updates, and follow news, trends, or public figures. In a way, it rolls conversation, entertainment, and information into one. That is why so many people dip in and out of it throughout the day without even thinking twice.
components.chinese: 原因其實很簡單：社交媒體已經變成一種數碼化的聚會場所。人們用它來和朋友聊天、發照片、回應別人的更新，也用來追蹤新聞、潮流，或者公眾人物。某程度上，它把交流、娛樂和資訊都捲在了一起。這也就是為甚麼很多人一天之中會不知不覺地一直進進出出地使用它。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is quite simple: social media has become a kind of
digital meeting place. People use it to talk to friends, post photos, react to other
people’s updates, and follow news, trends, or public figures. In a way, it rolls
conversation, entertainment, and information into one. That is why so many
people dip in and out of it throughout the day without even thinking twice.
[原因其實很簡單：社交媒體已經變成一種數碼化的聚會場所。人們用它來和朋友聊天、發
照片、回應別人的更新，也用來追蹤新聞、潮流，或者公眾人物。某程度上，它把交流、娛樂
和資訊都捲在了一起。這也就是為甚麼很多人一天之中會不知不覺地一直進進出出地使用

components.english: For example, someone might use social media in the morning to check the news, at lunchtime to message friends, and in the evening to watch short videos or share something funny. So it is not just one activity; it is more like a whole bundle of little activities living under the same roof.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人早上可能會用社交媒體看新聞，中午和朋友傳訊息，到了晚上則看短片，或者分享一些有趣的東西。所以，它其實不只是單一活動，而更像是一堆小活動住在同一個屋簷下。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone might use social media in the morning to
check the news, at lunchtime to message friends, and in the evening to watch
short videos or share something funny. So it is not just one activity; it is more
like a whole bundle of little activities living under the same roof.
[例如，一個人早上可能會用社交媒體看新聞，中午和朋友傳訊息，到了晚上則看短片，或
者分享一些有趣的東西。所以，它其實不只是單一活動，而更像是一堆小活動住在同一個屋
簷下。]

components.english: So, in general, people use social media to communicate, express themselves, and stay plugged into the wider world.
components.chinese: 所以，一般來說，人們使用社交媒體是為了溝通、表達自己，以及讓自己和更大的世界保持連接。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in general, people use social media to communicate, express
themselves, and stay plugged into the wider world.
[所以，一般來說，人們使用社交媒體是為了溝通、表達自己，以及讓自己和更大的世界保
持連接。]

components.english: On top of that, people can also use social media for more practical purposes, such as learning, promoting a business, building a personal brand, or supporting a cause.
components.chinese: 除此之外，人們也可以把社交媒體用在更實際的用途上，例如學習、推廣生意、建立個人形象，或者支持某個理念。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, people can also use social media for more practical
purposes, such as learning, promoting a business, building a personal brand, or
supporting a cause.
[除此之外，人們也可以把社交媒體用在更實際的用途上，例如學習、推廣生意、建立個人
形象，或者支持某個理念。]

components.english: These days, social media is not just a place to scroll for fun. It can also be a shop window, a classroom, a stage, or even a megaphone. Small businesses use it to reach customers, students use it to pick up ideas or explanations, and creators use it to show their work to the world. At the same time, people can raise awareness about social issues, organise events, or speak up about something they care about. In other words, social media can be light as air one moment and surprisingly powerful the next.
components.chinese: 如今，社交媒體已經不只是讓人隨手滑來消遣的地方。它也可以是一個展示櫥窗、一間課室、一個舞台，甚至是一支擴音器。小商戶用它接觸顧客，學生用它吸收想法或理解一些知識，而創作者則用它向世界展示自己的作品。與此同時，人們也可以透過它提升大家對社會議題的關注、組織活動，或者為自己在意的事情發聲。換句話說，社交媒體有時輕得像空氣，有時卻又出奇地有力量。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: These days, social media is not just a place to scroll for fun. It
can also be a shop window, a classroom, a stage, or even a megaphone. Small
businesses use it to reach customers, students use it to pick up ideas or
explanations, and creators use it to show their work to the world. At the same
time, people can raise awareness about social issues, organise events, or speak up
about something they care about. In other words, social media can be light as air
one moment and surprisingly powerful the next.
[如今，社交媒體已經不只是讓人隨手滑來消遣的地方。它也可以是一個展示櫥窗、一間課
室、一個舞台，甚至是一支擴音器。小商戶用它接觸顧客，學生用它吸收想法或理解一些知
識，而創作者則用它向世界展示自己的作品。與此同時，人們也可以透過它提升大家對社會
議題的關注、組織活動，或者為自己在意的事情發聲。換句話說，社交媒體有時輕得像空氣
，有時卻又出奇地有力量。]

components.english: For instance, a young designer might post their work online and attract clients, a fitness coach might share workout tips, or an ordinary user might help spread useful information during an emergency. So while some people use social media just to pass the time, others use it to open doors.
components.chinese: 例如，一位年輕設計師可以把作品放到網上，從而吸引客戶；一位健身教練可以分享運動建議；而一位普通用戶也可以在緊急情況下幫忙傳遞有用資訊。所以，有些人用社交媒體只是為了打發時間，但也有些人是用它來打開機會之門。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a young designer might post their work online and
attract clients, a fitness coach might share workout tips, or an ordinary user
might help spread useful information during an emergency. So while some
people use social media just to pass the time, others use it to open doors.
[例如，一位年輕設計師可以把作品放到網上，從而吸引客戶；一位健身教練可以分享運動
建議；而一位普通用戶也可以在緊急情況下幫忙傳遞有用資訊。所以，有些人用社交媒體只
是為了打發時間，但也有些人是用它來打開機會之門。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people can do almost anything on social media, from chatting and relaxing to learning, selling, and influencing others. It can be a playground, a marketplace, or a platform, depending on how people choose to use it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，人們在社交媒體上幾乎甚麼都可以做，從聊天、放鬆，到學習、做生意，以及影響他人都可以。它可以是一個遊樂場、一個市場，或者一個平台，端看人們選擇怎樣去使用它。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people can do almost anything on social media,
from chatting and relaxing to learning, selling, and influencing others. It can be
a playground, a marketplace, or a platform, depending on how people choose to
use it.
[總結來說，我會說，人們在社交媒體上幾乎甚麼都可以做，從聊天、放鬆，到學習、做生意
，以及影響他人都可以。它可以是一個遊樂場、一個市場，或者一個平台，端看人們選擇怎
樣去使用它。]

question.english: What about elderly people? Do elderly people use the Internet very much?
question.chinese: 那長者呢？長者會很常用互聯網嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: What about elderly people? Do elderly people use the Internet very much?
那長者呢？長者會很常用互聯網嗎？

components.english: I’d say more and more elderly people do use the Internet nowadays, but usually not as heavily or as instinctively as younger people.
components.chinese: 我會說，現在愈來愈多年長的人都會使用互聯網，不過一般來說，他們通常不像年輕人那樣用得那麼頻密，也沒有那麼自然。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say more and more elderly people do use the Internet nowadays, but
usually not as heavily or as instinctively as younger people.
[我會說，現在愈來愈多年長的人都會使用互聯網，不過一般來說，他們通常不像年輕人那
樣用得那麼頻密，也沒有那麼自然。]

components.english: The main reason is that younger people have grown up with the Internet, whereas many older people have had to learn it later in life. That makes a big difference. If something has not been part of your world from the very beginning, it rarely feels as natural as breathing. So, although many elderly people can and do use the Internet, they often use it more selectively and a little more cautiously.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，年輕人幾乎是伴隨著互聯網長大的，但很多長者卻是在較後的人生階段才開始接觸它。這會帶來很大的分別。如果一樣東西從一開始就不是你生活世界的一部分，它通常就不會像呼吸那樣自然。所以，雖然很多長者的確會上網，也懂得使用，但他們很多時候會用得更有選擇，也更小心一點。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that younger people have grown up with the
Internet, whereas many older people have had to learn it later in life. That
makes a big difference. If something has not been part of your world from the
very beginning, it rarely feels as natural as breathing. So, although many elderly
people can and do use the Internet, they often use it more selectively and a little
more cautiously.
[主要原因是，年輕人幾乎是伴隨著互聯網長大的，但很多長者卻是在較後的人生階段才開
始接觸它。這會帶來很大的分別。如果一樣東西從一開始就不是你生活世界的一部分，它通
常就不會像呼吸那樣自然。所以，雖然很多長者的確會上網，也懂得使用，但他們很多時候
會用得更有選擇，也更小心一點。]

components.english: For example, many older people are happy to use the Internet for practical and meaningful things, such as sending messages, making video calls, reading the news, or watching videos. So they are not cut off from the digital world; they just do not always live in it the way younger people do.
components.chinese: 例如，很多長者都很樂意用互聯網來做一些實際而有意義的事情，例如發訊息、視像通話、看新聞，或者看影片。所以，他們並不是和數碼世界完全脫節；他們只是未必像年輕人那樣整個人都活在網絡裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, many older people are happy to use the Internet for
practical and meaningful things, such as sending messages, making video calls,
reading the news, or watching videos. So they are not cut off from the digital
world; they just do not always live in it the way younger people do.
[例如，很多長者都很樂意用互聯網來做一些實際而有意義的事情，例如發訊息、視像通
話、看新聞，或者看影片。所以，他們並不是和數碼世界完全脫節；他們只是未必像年輕人
那樣整個人都活在網絡裡。]

components.english: So yes, many elderly people do use the Internet, but in general I would not say they use it very heavily.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，很多長者的確會使用互聯網，但整體來說，我不會說他們用得非常重。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, many elderly people do use the Internet, but in general I
would not say they use it very heavily.
[所以，是的，很多長者的確會使用互聯網，但整體來說，我不會說他們用得非常重。]

components.english: That said, I do not think age itself is the real barrier; very often, the real issue is confidence.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為年齡本身才是真正的障礙；很多時候，真正的問題其實是信心。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think age itself is the real barrier; very often, the
real issue is confidence.
[不過，我並不認為年齡本身才是真正的障礙；很多時候，真正的問題其實是信心。]

components.english: To be honest, a lot of older people are not unwilling to use the Internet; they are simply worried about doing something wrong. They may be afraid of scams, complicated apps, forgotten passwords, or pressing the wrong button and getting hopelessly lost. In other words, it is often less about resistance and more about uncertainty. Once that fear is reduced, many of them actually pick things up better than people expect.
components.chinese: 老實說，很多長者並不是不願意使用互聯網；他們只是擔心自己會出錯。他們可能害怕遇上騙案、應用程式太複雜、忘記密碼，或者按錯按鈕之後就完全不知道怎樣回頭。換句話說，很多時候問題與其說是抗拒，不如說是不確定感。一旦那份害怕減少了，其實很多長者都比人們想像中學得更好。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, a lot of older people are not unwilling to use the
Internet; they are simply worried about doing something wrong. They may be
afraid of scams, complicated apps, forgotten passwords, or pressing the wrong
button and getting hopelessly lost. In other words, it is often less about
resistance and more about uncertainty. Once that fear is reduced, many of them
actually pick things up better than people expect.
[老實說，很多長者並不是不願意使用互聯網；他們只是擔心自己會出錯。他們可能害怕遇
上騙案、應用程式太複雜、忘記密碼，或者按錯按鈕之後就完全不知道怎樣回頭。換句話說
，很多時候問題與其說是抗拒，不如說是不確定感。一旦那份害怕減少了，其實很多長者都
比人們想像中學得更好。]

components.english: For instance, if a grandparent is shown patiently how to use WhatsApp, YouTube, or online booking, they may become quite comfortable with it over time. Some even end up using those tools regularly, especially when it helps them stay in touch with family.
components.chinese: 例如，如果有人有耐性地一步一步教祖父母怎樣用 WhatsApp、YouTube，或者網上預約，他們很多時候慢慢就會變得很習慣。有些人甚至最後會很規律地使用這些工具，尤其是當它們能幫助自己和家人保持聯繫的時候。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a grandparent is shown patiently how to use
WhatsApp, YouTube, or online booking, they may become quite comfortable
with it over time. Some even end up using those tools regularly, especially when
it helps them stay in touch with family.
[例如，如果有人有耐性地一步一步教祖父母怎樣用 WhatsApp、YouTube，或者網上預約，
他們很多時候慢慢就會變得很習慣。有些人甚至最後會很規律地使用這些工具，尤其是當
它們能幫助自己和家人保持聯繫的時候。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say elderly people do use the Internet, but usually in a more careful and purposeful way. They may not dive into it headfirst like the young, but that does not mean they cannot adapt; very often, they just need a little more time and a little more patience.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說長者的確會使用互聯網，只不過通常用得更謹慎，也更有目的性。他們也許不會像年輕人那樣一頭栽進去，但那並不代表他們不能適應；很多時候，他們只是需要多一點時間，以及多一點耐性。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say elderly people do use the Internet, but usually
in a more careful and purposeful way. They may not dive into it headfirst like
the young, but that does not mean they cannot adapt; very often, they just need
a little more time and a little more patience.
[總結來說，我會說長者的確會使用互聯網，只不過通常用得更謹慎，也更有目的性。他們
也許不會像年輕人那樣一頭栽進去，但那並不代表他們不能適應；很多時候，他們只是需要
多一點時間，以及多一點耐性。]

question.english: Do you think older people and younger people will use the same kind of social media software?
question.chinese: 主題：你認為年長人士和年輕人將來會使用同一類社交媒體軟件嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think older people and younger people will use the same kind of social media software?
[主題：你認為年長人士和年輕人將來會使用同一類社交媒體軟件嗎？]

components.english: To some extent, yes, I think they will, especially when a platform becomes simple, useful, and widely accepted.
components.chinese: 在某程度上，我認為會的，尤其是當某個平台變得簡單、實用，而且被廣泛接受的時候。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: To some extent, yes, I think they will, especially when a platform
becomes simple, useful, and widely accepted.
[在某程度上，我認為會的，尤其是當某個平台變得簡單、實用，而且被廣泛接受的時候。]

components.english: The reason is that once a piece of social media becomes easy to use and genuinely helpful, it often spreads beyond one age group. Older people may start using it to keep in touch with family, see photos, join community groups, or follow news and hobbies, while younger people may already be there for socialising and entertainment. In that sense, social media often begins as a youth trend but ends up becoming part of everyday life for everyone. What starts in the hands of the young can gradually land on everyone’s phone.
components.chinese: 原因是，當一個社交平台變得容易使用，而且真的對人有幫助時，它往往就會超越單一年齡層。年長人士可能會開始用它來和家人聯絡、看照片、參加社區群組，或者追蹤新聞和興趣內容；而年輕人則可能本來就已經在上面社交和娛樂。從這個角度來說，社交媒體往往一開始是年輕人的潮流，但最後卻會變成所有人日常生活的一部分。一樣本來只在年輕人手中的東西，慢慢也可能落到每個人的手機裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that once a piece of social media becomes easy to
use and genuinely helpful, it often spreads beyond one age group. Older people
may start using it to keep in touch with family, see photos, join community
groups, or follow news and hobbies, while younger people may already be there
for socialising and entertainment. In that sense, social media often begins as a
youth trend but ends up becoming part of everyday life for everyone. What
starts in the hands of the young can gradually land on everyone’s phone.
[原因是，當一個社交平台變得容易使用，而且真的對人有幫助時，它往往就會超越單一年
齡層。年長人士可能會開始用它來和家人聯絡、看照片、參加社區群組，或者追蹤新聞和興
趣內容；而年輕人則可能本來就已經在上面社交和娛樂。從這個角度來說，社交媒體往往一
開始是年輕人的潮流，但最後卻會變成所有人日常生活的一部分。一樣本來只在年輕人手
中的東西，慢慢也可能落到每個人的手機裡。]

components.english: For example, if a family mostly communicates through one app, older relatives often start using the same platform simply because that is where the conversation is. They may join at first just to keep up with children or grandchildren, but over time they can become quite comfortable with it.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個家庭主要透過某一個應用程式溝通，年長的親人往往也會開始使用同一個平台，因為大家的交流都在那裡進行。他們一開始也許只是為了跟上子女或孫輩，但久而久之，他們也可能會用得相當習慣。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a family mostly communicates through one app,
older relatives often start using the same platform simply because that is where
the conversation is. They may join at first just to keep up with children or
grandchildren, but over time they can become quite comfortable with it.
[例如，如果一個家庭主要透過某一個應用程式溝通，年長的親人往往也會開始使用同一個
平台，因為大家的交流都在那裡進行。他們一開始也許只是為了跟上子女或孫輩，但久而久
之，他們也可能會用得相當習慣。]

components.english: So yes, I do think there will be some overlap, because useful technology tends to bring different generations onto the same platform sooner or later.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為兩代人之間會有一些重疊，因為真正有用的科技，遲早都會把不同世代帶到同一個平台上。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think there will be some overlap, because useful
technology tends to bring different generations onto the same platform sooner
or later.
[所以，是的，我的確認為兩代人之間會有一些重疊，因為真正有用的科技，遲早都會把不
同世代帶到同一個平台上。]

components.english: That said, I do not think older people and younger people will use exactly the same social media in exactly the same way.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為年長人士和年輕人會完全使用一模一樣的社交媒體，而且用法也不會完全相同。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think older people and younger people will use
exactly the same social media in exactly the same way.
[不過，我並不認為年長人士和年輕人會完全使用一模一樣的社交媒體，而且用法也不會完
全相同。]

components.english: Younger people usually move faster. They tend to be more drawn to new features, short-form content, trends, and spaces where they can express identity or keep up with what is current. Older people, by contrast, are often more interested in platforms that feel stable, clear, and practical. Even when both generations use the same app, they may inhabit it differently: younger users may post, create, and interact more actively, whereas older users may read, browse, and keep in touch more quietly. So the software may overlap, but the habits often tell a different story. The same stage, but not quite the same dance.
components.chinese: 年輕人通常行得更快。他們往往更容易被新功能、短內容、潮流，以及那些可以表達自我或追上時代節奏的空間吸引。相比之下，年長人士通常更重視那些感覺穩定、清晰，而且實用的平台。即使兩代人都在用同一個應用程式，他們其實也可能像住在不同區域裡：年輕用戶可能會更主動地發帖、創作和互動，而年長用戶則可能較安靜地閱讀、瀏覽，或者維繫聯絡。所以，平台也許會重疊，但使用習慣往往會說出另一個故事。同一個舞台，卻未必跳著同一支舞。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Younger people usually move faster. They tend to be more
drawn to new features, short-form content, trends, and spaces where they can
express identity or keep up with what is current. Older people, by contrast, are
often more interested in platforms that feel stable, clear, and practical. Even
when both generations use the same app, they may inhabit it differently:
younger users may post, create, and interact more actively, whereas older users
may read, browse, and keep in touch more quietly. So the software may overlap,
but the habits often tell a different story. The same stage, but not quite the same
dance.
[年輕人通常行得更快。他們往往更容易被新功能、短內容、潮流，以及那些可以表達自我
或追上時代節奏的空間吸引。相比之下，年長人士通常更重視那些感覺穩定、清晰，而且實
用的平台。即使兩代人都在用同一個應用程式，他們其實也可能像住在不同區域裡：年輕用
戶可能會更主動地發帖、創作和互動，而年長用戶則可能較安靜地閱讀、瀏覽，或者維繫聯
絡。所以，平台也許會重疊，但使用習慣往往會說出另一個故事。同一個舞台，卻未必跳著
同一支舞。]

components.english: For instance, a teenager and a grandparent might both have the same app on their phone, but the teenager may use it for trends, humour, or social circles, while the older person may use it mainly to look at family updates or messages from friends. So from the outside it looks the same, but underneath, their reasons for using it can be worlds apart.
components.chinese: 例如，一個青少年和一位祖父母可能都在手機裡裝了同一個應用程式，但那位青少年可能是用來看潮流、幽默內容，或者和朋友圈互動；而年長者則可能主要是用來看家人的近況，或者和朋友傳訊息。所以，從表面看好像是一樣的，但實際上，他們使用它的原因可能相差很遠。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teenager and a grandparent might both have the
same app on their phone, but the teenager may use it for trends, humour, or
social circles, while the older person may use it mainly to look at family updates
or messages from friends. So from the outside it looks the same, but underneath,
their reasons for using it can be worlds apart.
[例如，一個青少年和一位祖父母可能都在手機裡裝了同一個應用程式，但那位青少年可能
是用來看潮流、幽默內容，或者和朋友圈互動；而年長者則可能主要是用來看家人的近況，
或者和朋友傳訊息。所以，從表面看好像是一樣的，但實際上，他們使用它的原因可能相差
很遠。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say older and younger people will probably share some of the same social media software, but not completely, and certainly not in the same spirit. Technology may bring generations onto the same screen, but age still shapes what they do once they get there.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說年長人士和年輕人大概會共享某些相同的社交媒體軟件，但不會完全一樣，而且他們使用時的心態也肯定不會完全相同。科技也許能把不同世代帶到同一塊螢幕前，但年齡仍然會決定他們到了那裡之後，究竟會怎樣使用它。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say older and younger people will probably share
some of the same social media software, but not completely, and certainly not in
the same spirit. Technology may bring generations onto the same screen, but age
still shapes what they do once they get there.
[總結來說，我會說年長人士和年輕人大概會共享某些相同的社交媒體軟件，但不會完全一
樣，而且他們使用時的心態也肯定不會完全相同。科技也許能把不同世代帶到同一塊螢幕
前，但年齡仍然會決定他們到了那裡之後，究竟會怎樣使用它。]

question.english: Are traditional media like television and newspapers still useful?
question.chinese: 像電視和報紙這類傳統媒體仍然有用嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Are traditional media like television and newspapers still useful?
像電視和報紙這類傳統媒體仍然有用嗎？

components.english: Yes, definitely. I think traditional media like television and newspapers are still useful.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對如此。我認為像電視和報紙這類傳統媒體仍然有用。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely. I think traditional media like television and newspapers
are still useful.
[是的，絕對如此。我認為像電視和報紙這類傳統媒體仍然有用。]

components.english: One major reason is that they usually provide more verified information and more context. Social media can spread news at lightning speed, but half-truths can travel just as fast. By contrast, traditional media is often slower because it is edited, checked, and put into context. In that sense, social media often gives the first draft of events, whereas television and newspapers are more likely to give the edited version.
components.chinese: 其中一個主要原因是，它們通常能提供經過較多核實、而且更有前文後理的資訊。社交媒體傳播消息可以快得驚人，但半真半假的內容也一樣傳得飛快。相比之下，傳統媒體之所以較慢，往往正是因為它經過編輯、查證，以及整理脈絡。從這個角度來看，社交媒體很多時候像是在拋出事件的第一稿，而電視和報紙則更像是整理過後的版本。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: One major reason is that they usually provide more verified
information and more context. Social media can spread news at lightning speed,
but half-truths can travel just as fast. By contrast, traditional media is often
slower because it is edited, checked, and put into context. In that sense, social
media often gives the first draft of events, whereas television and newspapers are
more likely to give the edited version.
[其中一個主要原因是，它們通常能提供經過較多核實、而且更有前文後理的資訊。社交媒
體傳播消息可以快得驚人，但半真半假的內容也一樣傳得飛快。相比之下，傳統媒體之所以
較慢，往往正是因為它經過編輯、查證，以及整理脈絡。從這個角度來看，社交媒體很多時
候像是在拋出事件的第一稿，而電視和報紙則更像是整理過後的版本。]

components.english: For example, if there is an election, a public health issue, or an economic crisis, a newspaper report or a TV news segment often explains the background, includes expert views, and shows why the issue matters instead of just throwing a dramatic headline at people.
components.chinese: 例如，如果遇上選舉、公共衛生問題，或者經濟危機，一篇報章報道或一段電視新聞通常會交代背景、加入專家觀點，並說明事情為甚麼重要，而不只是丟出一個戲劇化的標題去吸引人。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if there is an election, a public health issue, or an
economic crisis, a newspaper report or a TV news segment often explains the
background, includes expert views, and shows why the issue matters instead of
just throwing a dramatic headline at people.
[例如，如果遇上選舉、公共衛生問題，或者經濟危機，一篇報章報道或一段電視新聞通常
會交代背景、加入專家觀點，並說明事情為甚麼重要，而不只是丟出一個戲劇化的標題去吸
引人。]

components.english: So yes, I would say traditional media is still useful, especially for people who want more than quick reactions and click-worthy fragments.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說傳統媒體仍然有用，尤其對那些想得到的不只是即時反應和吸睛碎片資訊的人來說更是如此。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say traditional media is still useful, especially for
people who want more than quick reactions and click-worthy fragments.
[所以，是的，我會說傳統媒體仍然有用，尤其對那些想得到的不只是即時反應和吸睛碎片
資訊的人來說更是如此。]

components.english: That said, traditional media is not as dominant as it used to be, because people’s habits have changed dramatically.
components.chinese: 不過，傳統媒體已經不像以前那樣佔主導地位了，因為人們接收資訊的習慣已經大大改變。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, traditional media is not as dominant as it used to be,
because people’s habits have changed dramatically.
[不過，傳統媒體已經不像以前那樣佔主導地位了，因為人們接收資訊的習慣已經大大改

components.english: To be honest, many people, especially younger ones, want information instantly and on their phones, so fixed TV schedules and printed newspapers can feel a little old-school. If traditional media does not adapt, it can easily fade into the background. In other words, it still has real value, but it can no longer live on reputation alone.
components.chinese: 老實說，很多人，尤其是年輕人，都希望資訊可以即時出現，而且直接在手機上看到，所以固定時段的電視新聞和紙本報紙有時難免會讓人覺得有點過時。如果傳統媒體不懂得調整，它就很容易慢慢退到背景裡。換句話說，它仍然有真正的價值，但已經不能只靠昔日名聲撐下去。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, many people, especially younger ones, want
information instantly and on their phones, so fixed TV schedules and printed
newspapers can feel a little old-school. If traditional media does not adapt, it can
easily fade into the background. In other words, it still has real value, but it can
no longer live on reputation alone.
[老實說，很多人，尤其是年輕人，都希望資訊可以即時出現，而且直接在手機上看到，所以
固定時段的電視新聞和紙本報紙有時難免會讓人覺得有點過時。如果傳統媒體不懂得調整
，它就很容易慢慢退到背景裡。換句話說，它仍然有真正的價值，但已經不能只靠昔日名聲
撐下去。]

components.english: For instance, someone may first see a breaking story on social media, then turn to a newspaper website or a TV channel later to check whether it is actually accurate. That shows traditional media may no longer break the news first, but it still helps people separate real information from noise.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能先在社交媒體上看到突發新聞，之後再去看報章網站或電視頻道，確認事情到底是否準確。這就說明傳統媒體也許不再是第一個把新聞帶出來的地方，但它仍然能幫助人分清哪些是真資訊，哪些只是雜音。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone may first see a breaking story on social media,
then turn to a newspaper website or a TV channel later to check whether it is
actually accurate. That shows traditional media may no longer break the news
first, but it still helps people separate real information from noise.
[例如，一個人可能先在社交媒體上看到突發新聞，之後再去看報章網站或電視頻道，確認
事情到底是否準確。這就說明傳統媒體也許不再是第一個把新聞帶出來的地方，但它仍然
能幫助人分清哪些是真資訊，哪些只是雜音。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say television and newspapers are still useful, but their role has changed. They may no longer dominate the conversation, yet they still matter because they offer depth, structure, and a cooler head in a very noisy world.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說電視和報紙仍然有用，只是它們的角色已經改變了。它們也許不再主導整個對話，但在這個很吵雜的世界裡，它們仍然重要，因為它們能提供深度、結構，以及較冷靜的視角。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say television and newspapers are still useful, but
their role has changed. They may no longer dominate the conversation, yet they
still matter because they offer depth, structure, and a cooler head in a very noisy
world.
[總結來說，我會說電視和報紙仍然有用，只是它們的角色已經改變了。它們也許不再主導
整個對話，但在這個很吵雜的世界裡，它們仍然重要，因為它們能提供深度、結構，以及較
冷靜的視角。]

question.english: What are the advantages and disadvantages of using social media?
question.chinese: 主題：使用社交媒體有甚麼優點和缺點？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the advantages and disadvantages of using social media?
主題：使用社交媒體有甚麼優點和缺點？

components.english: I think social media has some very clear advantages, especially when it comes to communication, convenience, and access to information.
components.chinese: 我認為，社交媒體確實有一些很明顯的優點，尤其是在溝通、便利性，以及獲取資訊方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think social media has some very clear advantages, especially when it
comes to communication, convenience, and access to information.
[我認為，社交媒體確實有一些很明顯的優點，尤其是在溝通、便利性，以及獲取資訊方

components.english: The main reason is that it allows people to stay connected almost instantly, no matter where they are. Friends can keep in touch, families can share updates, and people can follow news, ideas, or communities that matter to them. In that sense, social media can make the world feel smaller and more connected. It is not just a place to post photos; it can also be a space for learning, networking, and finding support.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，它讓人們無論身在何處，都幾乎可以即時保持聯繫。朋友之間可以繼續交流，家人可以分享近況，而人們也可以追蹤自己在意的新聞、想法，或者社群。從這個角度來看，社交媒體會令世界感覺更小，也更緊密。它不只是拿來發照片的地方；它同時也可以是一個學習、建立人脈，以及尋找支持的空間。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that it allows people to stay connected
almost instantly, no matter where they are. Friends can keep in touch, families
can share updates, and people can follow news, ideas, or communities that
matter to them. In that sense, social media can make the world feel smaller and
more connected. It is not just a place to post photos; it can also be a space for
learning, networking, and finding support.
[主要原因是，它讓人們無論身在何處，都幾乎可以即時保持聯繫。朋友之間可以繼續交流
，家人可以分享近況，而人們也可以追蹤自己在意的新聞、想法，或者社群。從這個角度來
看，社交媒體會令世界感覺更小，也更緊密。它不只是拿來發照片的地方；它同時也可以是
一個學習、建立人脈，以及尋找支持的空間。]

components.english: For example, a student can use social media to stay in touch with classmates, follow educational pages, or even learn useful skills from short videos and online communities. In the same way, someone with relatives abroad can use it to keep that relationship alive instead of letting distance create silence.
components.chinese: 例如，一位學生可以透過社交媒體和同學保持聯絡、追蹤一些教育類專頁，甚至從短片或網上社群中學到實用技能。同樣地，如果一個人的親人住在外國，他也可以透過社交媒體維繫感情，而不會讓距離慢慢把關係變得沉默。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a student can use social media to stay in touch with
classmates, follow educational pages, or even learn useful skills from short videos
and online communities. In the same way, someone with relatives abroad can use
it to keep that relationship alive instead of letting distance create silence.
[例如，一位學生可以透過社交媒體和同學保持聯絡、追蹤一些教育類專頁，甚至從短片或
網上社群中學到實用技能。同樣地，如果一個人的親人住在外國，他也可以透過社交媒體維
繫感情，而不會讓距離慢慢把關係變得沉默。]

components.english: So, on the positive side, social media can be a very useful tool, because it helps people connect, learn, and stay in the loop more easily.
components.chinese: 所以，從正面的角度來看，社交媒體可以是一個很有用的工具，因為它能幫助人們更容易與他人連結、學習新事物，以及掌握周遭資訊。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, on the positive side, social media can be a very useful tool,
because it helps people connect, learn, and stay in the loop more easily.
[所以，從正面的角度來看，社交媒體可以是一個很有用的工具，因為它能幫助人們更容易
與他人連結、學習新事物，以及掌握周遭資訊。]

components.english: That said, I think the disadvantages are also quite serious, especially when social media is used too much or without much critical thinking.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為它的缺點同樣相當明顯，尤其當人們過度使用，或者缺乏批判思考地去使用它的時候。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I think the disadvantages are also quite serious, especially
when social media is used too much or without much critical thinking.
[不過，我也認為它的缺點同樣相當明顯，尤其當人們過度使用，或者缺乏批判思考地去使
用它的時候。]

components.english: To be honest, social media can be a double-edged sword. On the one hand, it connects people; on the other, it can distract them, drain their time, and even affect their mental well-being. The endless scroll can quietly eat away at hours of the day, and the constant stream of polished photos and highlight reels can make ordinary life seem dull by comparison. On top of that, false information can spread like wildfire if people share things without checking them first.
components.chinese: 老實說，社交媒體其實是一把雙刃劍。一方面，它讓人與人之間更容易連結；但另一方面，它也可能令人分心、消耗大量時間，甚至影響心理狀態。那種無止境地滑下去的感覺，會在不知不覺間吞掉一天裡很多時間；而那些經過修飾的照片和只展示光鮮片段的內容，也很容易令人覺得自己的普通生活相形見絀。除此之外，如果人們未經查證就隨手轉發，錯誤資訊也可以像野火一樣迅速蔓延。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, social media can be a double-edged sword. On the
one hand, it connects people; on the other, it can distract them, drain their time,
and even affect their mental well-being. The endless scroll can quietly eat away
at hours of the day, and the constant stream of polished photos and highlight
reels can make ordinary life seem dull by comparison. On top of that, false
information can spread like wildfire if people share things without checking
them first.
[老實說，社交媒體其實是一把雙刃劍。一方面，它讓人與人之間更容易連結；但另一方面，
它也可能令人分心、消耗大量時間，甚至影響心理狀態。那種無止境地滑下去的感覺，會在
不知不覺間吞掉一天裡很多時間；而那些經過修飾的照片和只展示光鮮片段的內容，也很
容易令人覺得自己的普通生活相形見絀。除此之外，如果人們未經查證就隨手轉發，錯誤資
訊也可以像野火一樣迅速蔓延。]

components.english: For instance, a teenager may go online just to check one message, but end up spending two hours scrolling through videos and comparing their life with other people’s. By the end of it, they may not feel entertained at all — just tired, distracted, and a little worse about themselves.
components.chinese: 例如，一個青少年本來可能只是想上網看一則訊息，但最後卻花了兩個小時不停滑影片，還順便拿自己的人生和別人比較。到最後，他未必真的覺得被娛樂到了，反而可能只剩下疲累、分心，甚至對自己感覺更差了一點。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teenager may go online just to check one message,
but end up spending two hours scrolling through videos and comparing their
life with other people’s. By the end of it, they may not feel entertained at all —
just tired, distracted, and a little worse about themselves.
[例如，一個青少年本來可能只是想上網看一則訊息，但最後卻花了兩個小時不停滑影片，
還順便拿自己的人生和別人比較。到最後，他未必真的覺得被娛樂到了，反而可能只剩下疲
累、分心，甚至對自己感覺更差了一點。]

components.english: Overall, I would say social media has both strong advantages and real drawbacks. It can open doors, but it can also pull people off track. So, in the end, the real issue is not whether social media is good or bad in itself, but whether people know how to use it with balance, judgment, and self-control.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說社交媒體既有很明顯的好處，也有很真實的壞處。它可以為人打開很多門，但也可能把人拉離正軌。所以說到底，真正的問題並不只是社交媒體本身是好是壞，而是人們是否懂得用平衡感、判斷力，以及自制力去使用它。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say social media has both strong advantages
and real drawbacks. It can open doors, but it can also pull people off track. So,
in the end, the real issue is not whether social media is good or bad in itself, but
whether people know how to use it with balance, judgment, and self-control.
[總結來說，我會說社交媒體既有很明顯的好處，也有很真實的壞處。它可以為人打開很多
門，但也可能把人拉離正軌。所以說到底，真正的問題並不只是社交媒體本身是好是壞，而
是人們是否懂得用平衡感、判斷力，以及自制力去使用它。]

question.english: Do older people spend much time on social media?
question.chinese: 長者會花很多時間在社交媒體上嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do older people spend much time on social media?
長者會花很多時間在社交媒體上嗎？

components.english: I’d say some of them do, yes, but usually not in quite the same way as younger people.
components.chinese: 我會說，有些長者的確會花不少時間在社交媒體上，不過通常不會像年輕人那樣使用。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say some of them do, yes, but usually not in quite the same way as
younger people.
[我會說，有些長者的確會花不少時間在社交媒體上，不過通常不會像年輕人那樣使用。]

components.english: These days, a lot of older people use social media to keep in touch with family, look at photos, watch short videos, or follow the news. So it is definitely part of their daily life now. That said, many of them tend to use it more purposefully. They are more likely to go online for a reason, check a few things, and then come off, rather than scroll endlessly just for the sake of it.
components.chinese: 現在很多長者會用社交媒體和家人聯絡、看看照片、看短片，或者留意新聞，所以它的確已經成為他們日常生活的一部分。不過，很多長者使用社交媒體時往往比較有目的性。他們通常比較像是為了某個原因才上去看一看、查一查，之後就離開，而不是單純不停地滑下去、沒完沒了地看。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: These days, a lot of older people use social media to keep in
touch with family, look at photos, watch short videos, or follow the news. So it
is definitely part of their daily life now. That said, many of them tend to use it
more purposefully. They are more likely to go online for a reason, check a few
things, and then come off, rather than scroll endlessly just for the sake of it.
[現在很多長者會用社交媒體和家人聯絡、看看照片、看短片，或者留意新聞，所以它的確
已經成為他們日常生活的一部分。不過，很多長者使用社交媒體時往往比較有目的性。他們
通常比較像是為了某個原因才上去看一看、查一查，之後就離開，而不是單純不停地滑下
去、沒完沒了地看。]

components.english: For example, an older person might use WhatsApp or Facebook to see what their children and grandchildren are doing, reply in a family group chat, or pass the time with a few videos in the evening. They may not be glued to their phone all day, but they can still spend a fair amount of time on it.
components.chinese: 例如，一位長者可能會用 WhatsApp 或 Facebook 看看子女和孫兒最近在做甚麼、在家庭群組裡回覆訊息，或者晚上看幾段短片消磨一下時間。他們未必會整天黏在手機上，但仍然可能花上不少時間。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, an older person might use WhatsApp or Facebook to
see what their children and grandchildren are doing, reply in a family group
chat, or pass the time with a few videos in the evening. They may not be glued
to their phone all day, but they can still spend a fair amount of time on it.
[例如，一位長者可能會用 WhatsApp 或 Facebook 看看子女和孫兒最近在做甚麼、在家庭
群組裡回覆訊息，或者晚上看幾段短片消磨一下時間。他們未必會整天黏在手機上，但仍然
可能花上不少時間。]

components.english: So yes, I think many older people do spend time on social media now, just in a more selective and practical way.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我覺得現在很多長者的確會花時間在社交媒體上，只不過他們的使用方式通常比較有選擇，也比較實際。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I think many older people do spend time on social media
now, just in a more selective and practical way.
[所以，是的，我覺得現在很多長者的確會花時間在社交媒體上，只不過他們的使用方式通
常比較有選擇，也比較實際。]

components.english: That said, it really depends on the person, because older people are not all cut from the same cloth.
components.chinese: 不過，這其實很看個人，因為長者也不是每一個都一模一樣。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, it really depends on the person, because older people are not
all cut from the same cloth.
[不過，這其實很看個人，因為長者也不是每一個都一模一樣。]

components.english: Some older people are very comfortable with technology and use social media every day, whereas others find it confusing, noisy, or simply unnecessary. In other words, there is quite a wide spectrum. Some take to it like a duck to water, while others would still rather pick up the phone or talk face to face.
components.chinese: 有些長者對科技很熟悉，幾乎每天都會用社交媒體；但也有些人會覺得它太複雜、太吵，或者根本沒有太大需要。換句話說，這當中其實有很大的差異。有些人適應得很自然、很快，但也有些人仍然寧願直接打電話，或者面對面聊天。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some older people are very comfortable with technology and
use social media every day, whereas others find it confusing, noisy, or simply
unnecessary. In other words, there is quite a wide spectrum. Some take to it like
a duck to water, while others would still rather pick up the phone or talk face to
face.
[有些長者對科技很熟悉，幾乎每天都會用社交媒體；但也有些人會覺得它太複雜、太吵，
或者根本沒有太大需要。換句話說，這當中其實有很大的差異。有些人適應得很自然、很快
，但也有些人仍然寧願直接打電話，或者面對面聊天。]

components.english: For instance, one grandparent may happily send voice messages, watch cooking videos, and comment on family photos every day, while another may only use social media once in a while and prefer more traditional ways of keeping in touch.
components.chinese: 例如，一位祖父母可能每天都很樂意發語音訊息、看煮食影片，也會在家人的照片下面留言；但另一位長者卻可能只是偶爾才用一下社交媒體，並且更喜歡用較傳統的方式和別人聯絡。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, one grandparent may happily send voice messages,
watch cooking videos, and comment on family photos every day, while another
may only use social media once in a while and prefer more traditional ways of
keeping in touch.
[例如，一位祖父母可能每天都很樂意發語音訊息、看煮食影片，也會在家人的照片下面留
言；但另一位長者卻可能只是偶爾才用一下社交媒體，並且更喜歡用較傳統的方式和別人
聯絡。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say older people do use social media quite a bit nowadays, but not all of them spend a huge amount of time on it. A lot depends on personality, habit, and whether they see it as a useful tool or just background noise.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說現在長者的確會用不少社交媒體，但並不是每一位都會花很多很多時間在上面。這很大程度上取決於個人性格、生活習慣，以及他們究竟把社交媒體看成一個有用的工具，還是一些可有可無的背景雜音。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say older people do use social media quite a bit
nowadays, but not all of them spend a huge amount of time on it. A lot depends
on personality, habit, and whether they see it as a useful tool or just background
noise.
[總結來說，我會說現在長者的確會用不少社交媒體，但並不是每一位都會花很多很多時間
在上面。這很大程度上取決於個人性格、生活習慣，以及他們究竟把社交媒體看成一個有用
的工具，還是一些可有可無的背景雜音。]

question.english: How do you think AI (artificial intelligence) will affect people’s work?
question.chinese: 你認為人工智能會怎樣影響人們的工作？

source_lines.text: Topic: How do you think AI (artificial intelligence) will affect people’s work?
你認為人工智能會怎樣影響人們的工作？

components.english: I think AI will affect people’s work in a major way, mainly by taking over repetitive tasks and making many jobs faster, smarter, and more efficient.
components.chinese: 我認為，人工智能會在很大程度上影響人們的工作，主要是因為它會接手一些重複性的任務，令很多工作變得更快、更聰明，也更有效率。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think AI will affect people’s work in a major way, mainly by taking
over repetitive tasks and making many jobs faster, smarter, and more efficient.
[我認為，人工智能會在很大程度上影響人們的工作，主要是因為它會接手一些重複性的任
務，令很多工作變得更快、更聰明，也更有效率。]

components.english: The main reason is that a lot of work involves routine tasks that do not always need deep human judgment, such as sorting information, answering simple questions, drafting basic content, or spotting patterns in large amounts of data. AI is especially good at that kind of heavy lifting. If machines can handle the mechanical side of work, people may have more time to focus on creativity, decision-making, and human interaction. In that sense, AI could become less like a rival and more like a co-pilot.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，很多工作其實都包含一些例行而重複的部分，未必每一次都需要很深層的人類判斷，例如整理資訊、回答簡單問題、起草基本內容，或者從大量數據中找出模式。人工智能特別擅長處理這類「重活」。如果機器可以負責工作中較機械化的一面，人就可能有更多時間去專注於創意、決策，以及人與人之間的互動。從這個角度來說，人工智能與其說是對手，不如說更像一位副駕駛。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that a lot of work involves routine tasks that
do not always need deep human judgment, such as sorting information,
answering simple questions, drafting basic content, or spotting patterns in large
amounts of data. AI is especially good at that kind of heavy lifting. If machines
can handle the mechanical side of work, people may have more time to focus on
creativity, decision-making, and human interaction. In that sense, AI could
become less like a rival and more like a co-pilot.
[主要原因是，很多工作其實都包含一些例行而重複的部分，未必每一次都需要很深層的人
類判斷，例如整理資訊、回答簡單問題、起草基本內容，或者從大量數據中找出模式。人工
智能特別擅長處理這類「重活」。如果機器可以負責工作中較機械化的一面，人就可能有更
多時間去專注於創意、決策，以及人與人之間的互動。從這個角度來說，人工智能與其說是
對手，不如說更像一位副駕駛。]

components.english: For example, an office worker might use AI to summarise meeting notes, organise emails, or prepare a first draft of a report. That does not mean the person becomes unnecessary; rather, it means they can spend less time doing the groundwork and more time refining ideas or solving real problems.
components.chinese: 例如，一位辦公室員工可以利用人工智能去整理會議記錄、分類電郵，或者先起草一份報告的初稿。這並不代表那個人就變得沒有價值；更準確地說，這只是代表他可以少花時間做一些基礎工序，並把更多精力放在完善想法或解決真正的問題上。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, an office worker might use AI to summarise meeting
notes, organise emails, or prepare a first draft of a report. That does not mean
the person becomes unnecessary; rather, it means they can spend less time doing
the groundwork and more time refining ideas or solving real problems.
[例如，一位辦公室員工可以利用人工智能去整理會議記錄、分類電郵，或者先起草一份報
告的初稿。這並不代表那個人就變得沒有價值；更準確地說，這只是代表他可以少花時間做
一些基礎工序，並把更多精力放在完善想法或解決真正的問題上。]

components.english: So, overall, I would say AI is likely to reshape work by boosting productivity and changing the kind of tasks people spend their time on.
components.chinese: 所以，整體而言，我會說人工智能很可能會透過提升生產力，以及改變人們把時間花在哪些任務上，來重新塑造工作的樣貌。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I would say AI is likely to reshape work by boosting
productivity and changing the kind of tasks people spend their time on.
[所以，整體而言，我會說人工智能很可能會透過提升生產力，以及改變人們把時間花在哪
些任務上，來重新塑造工作的樣貌。]

components.english: That said, I do think AI will also create real challenges, because not everyone will benefit from it in the same way.
components.chinese: 不過，我也的確認為人工智能會帶來真實的挑戰，因為並不是每一個人都能以同樣的方式從中受益。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think AI will also create real challenges, because not
everyone will benefit from it in the same way.
[不過，我也的確認為人工智能會帶來真實的挑戰，因為並不是每一個人都能以同樣的方式
從中受益。]

components.english: Some jobs, especially those built around routine and predictable tasks, may shrink or change dramatically. That can leave people feeling uncertain, or even as if the ground is shifting under their feet. On top of that, workers may need to keep learning new tools just to stay relevant. In other words, AI may open new doors, but it may also close a few old ones. So the real issue is not simply whether AI is good or bad; it is whether people and systems can adapt quickly enough.
components.chinese: 有些工作，尤其是那些主要建立在例行和可預測任務之上的職位，可能會縮減，甚至出現很大的變化。這會令人感到不安，甚至有一種腳下的地面正在移動的感覺。除此之外，工作者也可能需要不斷學習新工具，才能讓自己不被淘汰。換句話說，人工智能也許會打開一些新的門，但同時也可能會關上幾扇舊的門。所以，真正的問題並不只是人工智能到底是好還是壞，而是人和制度能否夠快地作出調整。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some jobs, especially those built around routine and
predictable tasks, may shrink or change dramatically. That can leave people
feeling uncertain, or even as if the ground is shifting under their feet. On top of
that, workers may need to keep learning new tools just to stay relevant. In other
words, AI may open new doors, but it may also close a few old ones. So the real
issue is not simply whether AI is good or bad; it is whether people and systems
can adapt quickly enough.

很大的變化。這會令人感到不安，甚至有一種腳下的地面正在移動的感覺。除此之外，工作
者也可能需要不斷學習新工具，才能讓自己不被淘汰。換句話說，人工智能也許會打開一些
新的門，但同時也可能會關上幾扇舊的門。所以，真正的問題並不只是人工智能到底是好還
是壞，而是人和制度能否夠快地作出調整。]

components.english: For instance, a customer service worker may find that AI can already handle many basic enquiries, so their role may shift towards dealing with more complicated or sensitive cases. That could be a good thing if they are trained and supported properly, but a difficult one if they are simply left to sink or swim.
components.chinese: 例如，一位客服人員可能會發現，人工智能已經可以處理很多基本查詢，所以他的工作角色可能會轉向處理更複雜、或者更需要同理心的個案。如果他能夠得到適當培訓和支援，這也許會是一件好事；但如果他只是被丟下去自生自滅，那就可能變成一件很困難的事。

already handle many basic enquiries, so their role may shift towards dealing
with more complicated or sensitive cases. That could be a good thing if they are
trained and supported properly, but a difficult one if they are simply left to sink
or swim.
[例如，一位客服人員可能會發現，人工智能已經可以處理很多基本查詢，所以他的工作角
色可能會轉向處理更複雜、或者更需要同理心的個案。如果他能夠得到適當培訓和支援，這
也許會是一件好事；但如果他只是被丟下去自生自滅，那就可能變成一件很困難的事。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say AI will not just change how people work, but also what society expects from workers. In the end, it is likely to be a tool of both opportunity and pressure: it can lighten the load, but it can also raise the bar.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人工智能不只會改變人們怎樣工作，還會改變整個社會對工作者的期待。說到底，它很可能會同時是一種機會，也是一種壓力：它可以替人減輕負擔，但也會把要求提高。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say AI will not just change how people work, but
also what society expects from workers.

9. Conclusion 2: In the end, it is likely to be a tool of both opportunity and
pressure: it can lighten the load, but it can also raise the bar.
